As a (Senior) Data Analyst at Nelly, you'll transform fragmented health and finance data into scalable, trustworthy models, creating a single source of truth that powers company-wide strategic decisions. Your role goes beyond answering business questions: you'll own the core analytics logic, challenge requirements, and define how we measure business health.

About Nelly

At Nelly, we’re revolutionizing medical practice management with AI-driven automation and seamless digital workflows. Our innovative solutions - from best-in-class patient journeys, AI enabled patient relationship management and billing to state of the art payment solutions - eliminate painful workflows and improve financial outcomes making healthcare administration smarter, faster, and 100% digital.

To fuel our mission, we just raised €50M in our Series B, backed by top-tier investors like Cathay, Notion, Lakestar, GFC, b2venture, and Motive Partners. With this momentum, we’re on a mission to build Europe’s largest fintech in healthcare - bringing best-in-class financial and operational solutions to over 1 million medical practices serving millions of patients daily.

Join us now if you’re driven by impact, excited by fast-paced innovation, and ready to shape the future of healthcare!

Joining us, you will

  • Design, build, and maintain scalable data models using dbt and strong SQL, with a focus on robust data marts and a clean semantic layer.
  • Own the BI layer (Looker/LookML) end-to-end, creating self-service Explores that give the entire company a single source of truth for analysis.
  • Translate complex business logic into structured data models, including topics such as Customer Health Scores, NRR (cohort-based), CAC Payback, product unit economics, churn, provisions, and commercial KPIs.
  • Unify and harmonize data across systems such as Salesforce, Product Data, HubSpot, Jira, Intercom, Personio, and internal booking/factoring systems.
  • Develop end-to-end reporting solutions from requirement gathering and data validation to Looker dashboards and stakeholder-ready reports.

Build advanced analyses such as:

  • Sales funnel and GTM performance tracking
  • Commercial KPI automation
  • Cohort-based NRR, CAC payback and churn analytics
  • Revenue reconciliation (Signed vs. Actual Revenue)
  • Customer and usage benchmarks (including regional usage / booking patterns)

Ensuring data quality and cross-functional alignment:

  • Identify gaps in data collection and instrumentation before modeling begins, ensuring quality, consistency, and long-term scalability
  • Establish tests and validation logic in dbt to guarantee reliability and transparency across data models
  • Collaborate closely with Product, Commercial and Finance to deeply understand the processes generating the data
  • Support system alignment between tools to ensure consistent reporting logic across the organization

What You'll Bring

  • Strong hands-on experience with SQL, modular query design and dimensional data modeling (star schemas, fact/dimension design).
  • Production experience designing and maintaining scalable data models using modern transformation tools (e.g. dbt, SQLMesh, Metabase), including testing and documentation.
  • Experience developing SQL-based data marts and self-service explores BI-tool with modeling capabilities (e.g. Looker)
  • Solid understanding of revenue metrics (MRR, NRR, churn, cohort logic), SaaS KPIs, and GTM performance metrics.
  • Experience integrating and modeling data from tools such as Salesforce, HubSpot, CRM systems, and financial systems.
  • Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical frameworks.
  • A proactive mindset - you question definitions, challenge inconsistencies, and improve processes instead of just reporting on them.
  • Fluency in SQL; Experience in dbt and Looker/LookML is a plus
  • Professional proficiency in English; German is a plus.

What We Offer

  • Competitive salary
  • Work in a great team of talented and highly motivated people
  • Dynamic environment, steep learning curve, and extensive development opportunities
  • Part of an exciting AI enabled FinTech startup in the German healthcare market
  • Responsibility, independent work, and high flexibility
  • Generous budget for learning and development
  • First-class work equipment and flexible working hours
  • Deutschlandticket or Swapfiets rental bike
  • Urban Sports Club membership

We are looking forward to receiving your application and welcoming you to the team.

Title: Data Engineer, Fraud
Description:
Who Are We?

Xcelirate develops technologically-advanced platforms which are accessed by thousands of users every minute! We are proud to offer a workplace where the sharpest developers come together to strategically plan and swiftly execute practices which see us maintain our existing market dominance and attain global expansion. We owe our success to our customers who have seen us grow across a decade, and our talented team who have made that growth possible.

Who are we looking for?

The Data Engineer will focus on designing, developing, and maintaining robust data infrastructure to support use cases such as fraud detection but also general data engineering. The position emphasizes building scalable, high-performing data pipelines and storage systems for fraud use cases. Although this role involves light integration with machine learning models, its primary responsibility is creating the technical foundation that powers such use cases of fraud detection, analytics and reporting.

What will you be doing?


Develop and Maintain Pipelines: build and maintain efficient, scalable data pipelines for use cases such as fraud detection
Support Fraud Analytics: enable analysts and product teams to identify and address emerging fraud patterns through engineered datasets
Integrate Detection Models: collaborate with teams to operationalise external fraud detection models and integrate them into the data infrastructure
Data Storage Optimisation: design and optimise data storage solutions for analysing fraud signals and managing historical data
Feature Engineering: create fraud-specific datasets and features to enhance detection accuracy while supporting business and analytics teams
Pipeline Monitoring and Optimisation: monitor fraud data pipelines to ensure system reliability and troubleshoot performance issues
Best Practices Documentation: establish and document best practices for fraud-related data engineering
Cross-Team Collaboration: partner with data, product, and engineering teams to proactively address fraud trends


Requirements

What will you bring along?


3+ years of experience as a data engineer with some expertise in fraud detection systems or similar
Proficiency in Python and SQL, with knowledge of orchestration and transformation tools (e.g., Apache Airflow, DBT)
Strong knowledge of database design, query optimisation, and ETL/ELT workflows, ideally including relational databases such as MySQL and columnar databases such as ClickHouse
Experience building and maintaining production data pipelines
Familiarity with leveraging machine learning models, primarily as a component of the broader data pipeline, including model registries such as MLflow
Understanding of CI/CD processes for data pipelines
Hands-on experience with data visualization platforms for trend reporting (e.g., Tableau, Superset, Metabase)
Ability to support with model serving components, such as Flask or FastAPI
Familiarity with statistical techniques for fraud trend analysis and reporting
Experience with Git and version control in collaborative workflows


We are always looking for the best candidates, so if you think you would be a good fit even if you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we would love to hear from you!

Benefits

How We Support Our Contractors:


An annualised rate of €98,000 + retention bonus scheme: Competitive contractor rate with a generous bonus paid every 6 months based on your time with us
Top-Notch Workstation: We provide the latest MacBook, branded merchandise, and everything you need for an optimal work environment.
Global Co-Working Access: Work from a global network of co-working spaces to keep your work-life dynamic and flexible.
Flexibility: Enjoy full flexibility in work location and hours, supporting a work-life balance tailored to your needs.
Events and Gatherings: Participate in exciting events throughout the year, including team gatherings, cultural events, and other fun activities


At Xcelirate, we're committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender, gender identity or expression, or veteran status. We pride ourselves in being an equal opportunity workplace.

Title: Senior Marketing Data Analyst (all genders)
Description:
The role

As a Senior Marketing Data Analyst in our Global Marketing Analytics organisation, you will play a pivotal role in driving data-informed decision-making at HelloFresh. Based in our Berlin Headquarters, you’ll join the Market Insights team - an internal analytics function supporting senior leadership across 15+ HelloFresh markets spanning 3 continents.

You will turn data into strategic recommendations, challenge the status quo, and directly shape how our global brands grow and operate.

What You’ll Do


Act as a trusted advisor to Global Marketing leadership: understand their challenges, frame analytical problem statements, and translate insights into actionable strategic recommendations
Measure, benchmark, and monitor brand and market performance, proactively identifying opportunities to improve key Marketing and business KPIs
Influence and optimize Marketing budgets and planning processes by challenging assumptions and ensuring data-driven decision making
Lead high-impact analytical projects, often partnering directly with senior stakeholders to enhance performance frameworks, forecasting, growth strategies, and reporting ecosystems
Build and enhance reporting and visualization assets that support day-to-day decision-making and strengthen global performance oversight
Collaborate with and mentor analysts within the team, fostering a high-performing and supportive analytics community
Streamline analytical processes and workflows, championing scalable and automated solutions


What You’ll Bring


A motivated, collaborative mindset with strong team-working skills and a passion for solving complex business problems
5+ years of experience in Marketing Analytics, Finance Analytics, or a related quantitative field—ideally in a fast-paced B2C or subscription business
Strong understanding of Marketing and Growth metrics, and how they connect to overall business performance
A results-oriented and structured approach, able to turn vague or ambiguous business questions into clear analytical frameworks and insights
Solid technical skills, including:
Spreadsheets and SQL (required)
Tableau or a similar visualisation tool (e.g. Looker, PowerBI)
Python (a plus)
Proven project ownership and stakeholder management abilities, especially in senior or cross-functional settings
Excellent communication skills in English, with the ability to explain analytical concepts to non-technical audiences and influence decision-making

What we offer: The Toppings


Global collaboration at scale: Collaborate with experienced engineers and product partners across HelloTech’s international teams, in a culture of active knowledge sharing.
Technology with real-world impact: Build and operate modern systems at global scale, supporting 6+ millions of customers and complex supply chain operations.
Technical/Product/Design leadership: Drive best practices and influence architecture/design, quality, and ways of working in an autonomous, product-led setup.
End-to-end development/delivery: Drive decisions from problem definition to production, improving systems and enabling long-term scalability.
Access to workspace at Warsaw Centre Point. The hub offers modern facilities including showers, breakout zones, outdoor space, cycle parking, and refreshments (coffee, soft drinks, and fruit).


Are you up for the challenge?

Please submit your complete application below including your salary expectations and earliest starting date.

Title: Data Analyst - Teilportfolio AIDA
Description:
Data Analyst - Teilportfolio AIDA (m/w/d)

DKB AG

Standort: Berlin

unbefristet

Teil- & Vollzeit möglich

ab sofort

Deine Aufgaben


Du arbeitest im Teilportfolio AIDA an der Schnittstelle von Business, Data & AI, Data Engineering und Plattformentwicklung und machst Daten für Analytics-, Reporting-, KI- und Automatisierungs-Use-Cases nutzbar
Du wirkst am Aufbau einer produktorientierten Datenplattform mit und hilfst, Daten als Data Products verständlich, qualitätsgesichert und wiederverwendbar bereitzustellen
Du übersetzt fachliche Anforderungen in Datenanforderungen, Data-Product-Spezifikationen, Transformationslogiken und Abnahmekriterien und bist Sparringspartnerin für Product Owner*innen, Data Product Owner*innen und Fachbereiche
Du analysierst DKB-interne und externe Datenquellen, definierst fachliche Regeln für Integration, Harmonisierung und Qualitätssicherung und arbeitest an Data Products wie Person, Konto, Einkommen oder weiteren bankfachlichen Datenprodukten
Du schlägst die Brücke zwischen fachlichem Datenverständnis und technischer Implementierung, begleitest die Umsetzung in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Data Engineers und testest neue Datenprodukte, Datenlogiken und Plattformartefakte fachlich auf Herz und Nieren


Dein Profil


Du hast einen Abschluss in (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Statistik, Mathematik, Wirtschaftswissenschaften oder einem vergleichbaren quantitativen Studiengang und bringst relevante Berufserfahrung als Data Analyst*in oder Analytics Engineer mit
Du verstehst fachliche Anforderungen schnell, kannst sie strukturieren, priorisieren und in belastbare Datenanforderungen, Analysekonzepte und (technische) Umsetzungsschritte übersetzen
Du bringst sehr gute SQL-Kenntnisse mit, kannst Datenmodelle, Datenflüsse und Transformationslogiken sicher analysieren, validieren und fachlich bewerten und so analytische Fragestellungen von der Datenexploration bis zur Ergebnisaufbereitung begleiten
Begriffe wie Data Product, Data Contract, Data Product Factory und Data Marketplace sind für dich idealerweise keine Fremdwörter - oder du hast Lust, diese Themen aktiv mitzugestalten
Du arbeitest sicher mit Git, Jira und Confluence und bewegst dich gern in einem agilen, interdisziplinären Umfeld


Die DKB steht für Vielfalt und Chancengerechtigkeit. Egal, woher du kommst, wie alt du bist, welches Geschlecht, welche Genderidentität, sexuelle Orientierung oder Religion du hast, ob mit oder ohne Behinderung - wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung!

Deine Benefits und mehr


Attraktive Vergütung (tariflich oder außertariflich)
Flexibles und mobiles Arbeiten – temporär auch im EU-Ausland
Du hast die Wahl: Benefits Pass, Jobticket oder Jobrad
Moderne Technik- ausstattung und Tool Stack
Individuelle Qualifi- zierungsmöglichkeiten
Duz-Kultur und kein Dresscode
Externe Mitarbeitenden- beratung
Angebote zum Gesundbleiben
Weitere Extras wie z.B. betriebliche Altersvorsorge und Versicherungsschutz


Immer für dich ansprechbar

Axel Zimmermann

Chapter Lead

axel.zimmermann@dkb.de

Deutsche Kreditbank AG

Du möchtest mehr zur DKB erfahren?

Dann schau auf unserer Karriereseite vorbei.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About Huzzle

At Huzzle, we connect exceptional talents with top opportunities at leading companies across the UK, US, Canada, Europe, and Australia. Our clients include startups, digital agencies, and tech platforms in industries such as SaaS, MarTech, FinTech, and AI.

Unlike an outsourcing agency, we place you directly with a client where you're hired in-house as a valued member of their team.

Job Type: Full-time

Location: On-site in Berlin, Germany

About The Company

Our client is an ambitious, venture-backed startup building an AI-powered SAP consultant designed to transform how enterprise systems are implemented and optimized. Already post-revenue and working with DAX 40 and Fortune 500 companies. Their mission is to encode and compound consulting knowledge across deployments, creating a system that becomes smarter with every customer engagement.

Job Summary

We are hiring an AI Data Engineer to design and build the foundational data layer that powers a next-generation AI system for SAP consulting. This role is critical in structuring complex enterprise data—capturing configurations, decisions, and relationships across deployments—and transforming it into scalable, queryable knowledge that improves model accuracy over time.

You will work at the intersection of data engineering and AI, ensuring that structured insights flow seamlessly into machine learning systems and continuously evolve with each customer interaction.

Key Responsibilities


Design and build a scalable data layer that maps relationships across SAP projects, including configurations, outcomes, and domain patterns
Own the metadata architecture, including data labeling, linking, versioning, and queryability
Develop robust data pipelines to ingest SAP telemetry and consulting insights into structured datasets
Collaborate closely with AI/ML teams to provide high-quality signals for model training and inference
Continuously refine and improve data systems with each customer deployment
Ensure high standards of data integrity, consistency, and usability across systems
Architect systems with a long-term, scalable mindset rather than short-term fixes


Requirements


Strong background in data engineering, including pipeline development, schema design, and data modeling
Experience working with complex, semi-structured enterprise data
Knowledge of graph-based systems (knowledge graphs, graph databases) or relational modeling for complex domains
Systems-level thinking with a focus on architecture and scalability
Proven ability to maintain high standards for data quality and reliability
Willingness to work on-site in Berlin (mandatory requirement)


Benefits

💰 Competitive salary aligned with top-tier European startup benchmarks

🌍 Work on-site in Berlin at a fast-growing, venture-backed AI company

📈 High-growth environment with strong funding and enterprise traction

🤝 Collaborate with elite engineers, AI researchers, and enterprise clients

🧠 Exposure to cutting-edge AI, data systems, and enterprise-scale challenges

Title: Market Data Engineer
Description:
Location: Potsdam office, hybrid work model, applicants from Berlin and Brandenburg welcome

Language: Fluent in English




Company Description

Index Bakery is a financial technology company building modern infrastructure for the creation, calculation, and publication of financial indices. Our platform combines index engineering, market data processing, and operational tooling to help institutional clients launch and maintain index products efficiently and reliably.




We work at the intersection of finance, data, and software engineering. Our systems are designed to handle complex market data, support robust index calculations, and provide the transparency, auditability, and reliability required in a production financial environment.




As an early-stage company, we are building both the technology and the organization from the ground up. This creates a hands-on environment where engineers can take real ownership, contribute to core product decisions, and help shape how financial index infrastructure should work in the future.




Role Overview

We’re looking for a Market Data Engineer who thrives on transforming complex market data into clean, validated, and production-ready systems. You’ll be responsible for integrating data from multiple vendors, building robust data pipelines, and ensuring that every number can be trusted.




This role sits at the intersection of software engineering, financial market data, and data quality. You do not need to be a financial markets expert from day one, but you should be curious, structured, and eager to learn how market data, instruments, and index calculation processes work in production.




Why Join Us

At Index Bakery, you will work on systems that directly power the creation, calculation, and publication of financial indices used by institutional clients. We are a small and highly technical team where engineers have real ownership, direct impact, and the opportunity to shape both the platform and the company from an early stage.




As the company grows, you will have the opportunity to contribute not only to our systems, but also to the evolution of our data platform, products, and engineering practices.




Responsibilities

Integrate market data from external vendors, exchanges, and internal sources.
Build, maintain, and improve data ingestion pipelines for prices, reference data, FX rates, corporate actions, calendars, and other market data.
Normalize heterogeneous data formats into clean, consistent, and well-documented internal data models.
Implement validation checks, reconciliation logic, and data quality controls to detect missing, stale, inconsistent, or suspicious data.
Work closely with Index Engineering and Index Operations to ensure data is fit for backtesting, daily index calculation, reporting, and publication.
Investigate data issues in production and help identify root causes across vendors, pipelines, storage, and downstream systems.
Design pragmatic solutions for historical data management, versioning, corrections, and auditability of market data.
Contribute to internal tooling, APIs, monitoring, documentation, and operational workflows around market data.
Help shape the Market Data Platform as the company grows, balancing speed, reliability, and simplicity.




Requirements

3+ years of professional experience in software engineering, data engineering, or a closely related role.
Strong Python skills and the ability to write clean, maintainable, production-oriented code.
Solid SQL skills and practical experience working with relational databases.
Experience building or maintaining data pipelines, ETL/ELT processes, APIs, or backend services.
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure, ideally GCP, AWS, or Azure.
Understanding of data quality concepts such as validation, reconciliation, completeness checks, and auditability.
Ability to reason carefully about edge cases, data inconsistencies, and operational failure modes.
Comfortable working in a small team where ownership, communication, and pragmatic decision-making matter.
Fluent English communication skills.




Nice to Have

Experience with financial market data, index data, asset management, trading, exchanges, or data vendors.
Familiarity with instruments such as equities, bonds, ETFs, commodities, FX, derivatives, or crypto assets.
Experience with tools such as Docker, FastAPI, Pydantic, Airflow, Dagster, dbt, BigQuery, Cloud SQL, or similar technologies.
Experience with data historization, slowly changing dimensions, event-based data, or audit trails.
Experience operating production systems, including monitoring, alerting, incident analysis, and debugging.
Interest in financial markets, data modelling, and building systems where correctness matters.




What We Value

We care deeply about the mindset you bring to the team. We are looking for someone who is humble, curious, and eager to improve. Someone who enjoys learning new domains, asks thoughtful questions, and is comfortable saying, “I don’t know yet, but I’ll find out.”




We believe great engineers never stop learning. We value intellectual honesty, ownership, and collaboration. We are less interested in people who always have an answer, and more interested in people who are willing to challenge assumptions, learn from others, and continuously grow.




You should enjoy working through messy real-world data problems with patience and precision. We value people who take ownership without ego, communicate clearly, and want to build reliable systems together with a small, ambitious team.




What You'll Work On

At Index Bakery, market data is a core part of our infrastructure. Your work will directly support the calculation, validation, and publication of financial indices. You’ll help build the data foundation that enables index backtests, production calculations, client reports, operational checks, and future data products.




This is a hands-on engineering role with real ownership from the beginning.

Title: Junior Tracking Specialist (all genders)
Description:
Your role

You will support the measurement of user behavior and E-Commerce data on our websites and possible future app(s). For this, you will help continuously evolve the client- and server-side Google Tag Manager setup, including the usage of accompanying features in Google Cloud, as well as improving the GA4 setup.

The position centers around supporting to set up the best possible tracking to provide high-quality data to be used in our marketing channels, for website optimization, and in BI reporting to facilitate further business growth.

You will be an important contributor to the company's website data setup for our 10+ country domains together with our Senior Tracking Specialist.


Continuously improve the quality and quantity of measured data in GTM and GA 4
Support implementing data quality monitoring measures and creating suitable reports with Looker Studio
Participate in shaping our measurement strategy to meet marketing and business needs
Take over parts of the maintenance of Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics
Educate stakeholders about the features and limitations of our tracking setup
Continuously monitor new developments in the digital analytics ecosphere
Collaborate closely with our marketing and data teams to solve business questions, create synergies, and drive business growth through data
Support marketing teams with requests


Your profile


At least 1 year of in-depth experience with Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics 4; App tracking is a bonus
Demonstrated success in the development of tracking setups
Experience with setting up Server-Side GTM in a multi-domain setup
You are accustomed to the tracking needs of paid channels such as Google Ads, Meta Ads, and others
Basic knowledge in SQL, CSS, and JS is a plus
Strong focus on structured approaches, documentation, and organizing maintenance and monitoring of data quality
High interest in closely collaborating with Data and Marketing Teams
Talent to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
An interest in how AI will augment work in data measurement and digital analytics is a plus


What we offer


An appealing discount on all of our products – from essential oils to vitamins & nutrients
We offer 28 days of paid time off per year plus up to 5 days off between Christmas and New Year's (pro rata), provided Sunday Natural is closed for company holidays during this time
The Urban Sports Club membership and either Swapfiets bike rental or a subsidy for the Deutschlandticket
We believe great things happen when we meet in person, which is why we embrace a hybrid model with one home office day per week – up to two depending on the area you are working in – and up to two weeks per year working from abroad
Receive various discounts of up to more than 50 percent on attractive brands such as Flaconi, Bosch, Apple or Expedia
You will be given real ownership and plenty of room for new initiatives, as well as the opportunity to contribute your expertise and implement your great ideas
Inviting office spaces with access to plenty of organic teas and superfoods from around the world
We like to celebrate each other! Regular tea & office parties, Christmas and summer feasts and various influencer events such as Women's Day


Apply with your CV and cover letter.

Apply for this job

About Us

Sunday Natural emerged from a long-standing passion and research in the fields of health, healing, and personal growth. The lack of natural, high-quality products on the market was the original motivation for founding Sunday Natural in 2013. We aim to produce the products we ourselves would love to buy – as natural as possible, absolutely pure, free from any additives, and of the highest quality. In a strategic partnership with CVC, Sunday Natural is now one of Germany's most renowned quality manufacturers, with its own research and development department in Berlin. For the continuous improvement of our products, we collaborate with selected research institutes, scientists, therapists, farmers, and professional associations.

We take our ecological responsibility very seriously: that’s why the majority of our product range is vegan. We also take an innovative approach to packaging: most of our products are packaged in glass, making them almost entirely recyclable.

Our diverse team in Berlin consists of over 350 employees from more than 40 nationalities and continues to grow – but at a sustainable pace. Our goal is to foster a collaborative, supportive, and inclusive work environment with a diverse group of motivated, talented, and visionary team members.

We highly value equality of opportunity and inclusivity, and we would like to particularly encourage women and candidates from under-represented backgrounds to apply, even if you don’t match with 100% of the requirements.

Title: Working Student Operations & Analytics (x/f/m)
Description:
Gemeinsam gestalten wir die Gesundheitsversorgung der Zukunft

Doctolib sucht Dich ab sofort als Werkstudent:in im Bereich Operations & Analytics für unseren Standort in Berlin.  

Das LOE-Team steht für operative Exzellenz quer durch unsere kundenseitigen Bereiche – von Deployment über Customer Onboarding und Customer Success bis hin zu Support Excellence. Wir stellen sicher, dass alle Teams mit sauberen Daten, verlässlichen KPI-Reportings und reibungslosen CRM-Prozessen arbeiten können. So tragen wir dazu bei, dass Tausende Gesundheitseinrichtungen in Deutschland täglich die bestmögliche Unterstützung von Doctolib erhalten – und leisten damit unseren direkten Beitrag zu unserer Mission: den Zugang zur Gesundheitsversorgung für alle zu verbessern.

Deine Aufgaben

Als Werkstudent:in unterstützt Du uns in den folgenden Aufgabenbereichen:

Wöchentliche Datenexporte aus Salesforce, Metabase & Google Sheets
Manuelle KPI-Updates in Qobra (unserem Tool zur Bonusberechnung)
Pflege von Abwesenheits- und Headcount-Tracking-Tabellen
Aktualisierung von KPI-Präsentationen für die Teams Customer Onboarding & Deployment
Erstellung wiederkehrender Salesforce-Cases (KYC, Preiserhöhung, Churn)
Unterstützung bei der Bonus-Validierung und der Vorbereitung von Performance Reviews
Formatierung & Re-Import von Daten zwischen verschiedenen Systemen (z. B. Typeform → Salesforce, Google Sheets → Salesforce)
Ad-hoc-Unterstützung bei Reporting-Anfragen und Analytics-Tickets
Beantwortung von Datenfragen quer durch alle Teams (KPI-Definitionen, Berechnungen)
Während Deiner Tätigkeit bei uns wirst Du lernen:
relevanten Business-Intelligence- und CRM-Tools (Salesforce, Metabase, Qobra) in einem schnell wachsenden HealthTech-Unternehmen
Ein tiefes Verständnis für datengetriebene Operations-Prozesse entwickeln und verstehen, wie KPI-Systeme in einer internationalen Organisation funktionieren
Cross-funktionale Zusammenarbeit in einem dynamischen Umfeld erleben und ein breites Netzwerk innerhalb von Doctolib aufbauen
Dein Profil

Du bist der festen Überzeugung, dass Du genau das mitbringst, was es für die Position braucht? Dann ermutigen wir Dich, Dich zu bewerben. Selbst wenn Du nicht jede Anforderung erfüllst, bist Du vielleicht genau die/der richtige zukünftige Doctoliber:in für diese oder andere Stellen!

Um Teil unseres Teams zu werden, bringst Du Folgendes mit:
Du bist in Vollzeit an einer Hochschule immatrikuliert und studierst im Bereich BWL, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science, Statistik oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang (ab dem 3. Semester)
Du hast sehr gute Kenntnisse in Google Sheets oder Excel (Formeln, strukturierte Daten) und bist sicher im Umgang mit strukturierten Datensätzen
Du arbeitest strukturiert, präzise und zuverlässig – auch bei wiederkehrenden Aufgaben
Du beherrschst die Datenformatierung, -bereinigung sowie den Import/Export zwischen verschiedenen Systemen sicher
Du kommunizierst klar auf Englisch (working level) und informierst Dein Team proaktiv bei Problemen oder Unklarheiten
Besonders interessant sind für uns außerdem:
Erste Erfahrungen mit Salesforce (Exporte, Case-Erstellung, grundlegende Navigation)
Kenntnisse in Metabase oder einem ähnlichen BI-Tool
Deutschkenntnisse von Vorteil
Was wir Dir bieten
Ein strukturiertes und individuelles Onboarding im Rahmen unserer Doctolib Academy sowie ein breites Angebot interner Trainingsprogramme
Ein von Doctolib gesponsertes Deutschlandticket oder die Übernahme Deines Semestertickets
28 Urlaubstage im Jahr (anteilig auf Deine Arbeitszeiten berechnet)
Ein flexibles Arbeitsmodell, das hybrides und Office-basiertes Arbeiten ermöglicht
Regelmäßige Frühstücksangebote im Büro sowie Bezuschussungen zu Deinem Lebensmitteleinkauf
Ein gefördertes Sportangebot mit unserem Partner Urban Sports Club
Der Interviewprozess
Interview mit einer Deiner Managerin
Case Study mit Deinen Teammitgliedern
Offer
Job Details
Tätigkeit als Werkstudent:in (20 Std./Woche)
Tätigkeitsdauer: 3 Monate
Arbeitsort: Berlin
Gehalt: 15 €/Std.
Startdatum: zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt
Arbeitsmodus: Hybrid (2-3 Tage Office/Woche)



Wir verfolgen die Vision, die Gesundheit aller zu verbessern. Dies spiegelt sich auch im Recruiting wider. Wir evaluieren Bewerbungen ausschließlich auf der Grundlage von Qualifikation und Motivation. Diskriminierung wird bei uns nicht toleriert.

Je diverser unser Team und die Ideen sind, desto mehr wird unser Produkt die Gesundheit unserer Nutzer:innen verbessern können. Deshalb laden wir dich ein, dich bei uns zu bewerben, unabhängig von Geschlecht, Religion, Alter, sexueller Orientierung, ethnischer Herkunft oder Behinderung. 

Um Chancengleichheit zu gewährleisten, kannst Du in deiner Bewerbung auf Angaben wie Fotos, Alter, etc. verzichten. Lass uns bitte wissen, welche Vorkehrungen wir treffen können, um Dich bestmöglich zu unterstützen. Wir freuen uns, mit Dir das Gesundheitswesen zu digitalisieren!

Title: Robotics Data Collector (Germany)
Description:
CNTXT AI is a leading artificial intelligence company focused on building next-generation AI systems, products, and infrastructure. We specialize in developing multimodal AI, robotics, and intelligent agents, powered by high-quality real-world data. Data is at the core of our innovation. We invest heavily in data collection, annotation, and AI training pipelines to enable advanced systems such as embodied AI and Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.

Role Summary

CNTXT AI is looking for a Robotics Data Collector to support the collection of high-quality robotics and egocentric data used to train AI models.
This role involves working hands-on with robotic systems, sensors, and real-world environments to capture multimodal datasets, including video, motion, and interaction data. You will play a key role in enabling the development of intelligent robotic systems and AI agents.
The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, technically curious, and comfortable working in lab and controlled real-world environments.

Key Responsibilities

1. Data Collection Execution

Collect robotics and egocentric (first-person) data using:

Cameras (handheld, head-mounted, or robot-mounted)

Sensors (IMU, depth cameras, etc.)

Execute data collection tasks based on predefined scenarios and protocols.

Title: Junior Tracking Specialist (all genders)
Description:
Your role

You will support the measurement of user behavior and E-Commerce data on our websites and possible future app(s). For this, you will help continuously evolve the client- and server-side Google Tag Manager setup, including the usage of accompanying features in Google Cloud, as well as improving the GA4 setup.

The position centers around supporting to set up the best possible tracking to provide high-quality data to be used in our marketing channels, for website optimization, and in BI reporting to facilitate further business growth.

You will be an important contributor to the company's website data setup for our 10+ country domains together with our Senior Tracking Specialist.


Continuously improve the quality and quantity of measured data in GTM and GA 4
Support implementing data quality monitoring measures and creating suitable reports with Looker Studio
Participate in shaping our measurement strategy to meet marketing and business needs
Take over parts of the maintenance of Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics
Educate stakeholders about the features and limitations of our tracking setup
Continuously monitor new developments in the digital analytics ecosphere
Collaborate closely with our marketing and data teams to solve business questions, create synergies, and drive business growth through data
Support marketing teams with requests


Your profile


At least 1 year of in-depth experience with Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics 4; App tracking is a bonus
Demonstrated success in the development of tracking setups
Experience with setting up Server-Side GTM in a multi-domain setup
You are accustomed to the tracking needs of paid channels such as Google Ads, Meta Ads, and others
Basic knowledge in SQL, CSS, and JS is a plus
Strong focus on structured approaches, documentation, and organizing maintenance and monitoring of data quality
High interest in closely collaborating with Data and Marketing Teams
Talent to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
An interest in how AI will augment work in data measurement and digital analytics is a plus


What we offer


An appealing discount on all of our products – from essential oils to vitamins & nutrients
We offer 28 days of paid time off per year plus up to 5 days off between Christmas and New Year's (pro rata), provided Sunday Natural is closed for company holidays during this time
The Urban Sports Club membership and either Swapfiets bike rental or a subsidy for the Deutschlandticket
We believe great things happen when we meet in person, which is why we embrace a hybrid model with one home office day per week – up to two depending on the area you are working in – and up to two weeks per year working from abroad
Receive various discounts of up to more than 50 percent on attractive brands such as Flaconi, Bosch, Apple or Expedia
You will be given real ownership and plenty of room for new initiatives, as well as the opportunity to contribute your expertise and implement your great ideas
Inviting office spaces with access to plenty of organic teas and superfoods from around the world
We like to celebrate each other! Regular tea & office parties, Christmas and summer feasts and various influencer events such as Women's Day


Apply with your CV and cover letter.

About Us

Sunday Natural emerged from a long-standing passion and research in the fields of health, healing, and personal growth. The lack of natural, high-quality products on the market was the original motivation for founding Sunday Natural in 2013. We aim to produce the products we ourselves would love to buy – as natural as possible, absolutely pure, free from any additives, and of the highest quality. In a strategic partnership with CVC, Sunday Natural is now one of Germany's most renowned quality manufacturers, with its own research and development department in Berlin. For the continuous improvement of our products, we collaborate with selected research institutes, scientists, therapists, farmers, and professional associations.

We take our ecological responsibility very seriously: that’s why the majority of our product range is vegan. We also take an innovative approach to packaging: most of our products are packaged in glass, making them almost entirely recyclable.

Our diverse team in Berlin consists of over 350 employees from more than 40 nationalities and continues to grow – but at a sustainable pace. Our goal is to foster a collaborative, supportive, and inclusive work environment with a diverse group of motivated, talented, and visionary team members.

We highly value equality of opportunity and inclusivity, and we would like to particularly encourage women and candidates from under-represented backgrounds to apply, even if you don’t match with 100% of the requirements.

Title: Data Engineer (m/f/d) – Berlin
Description:
Your Mission

Your Impact

At Bees & Bears, data is the backbone of everything we do. As our first dedicated Data Engineer, you'll build the foundation that allows us to scale - cleanly, reliably, and without adding operational chaos.

Right now, our data lives in silos across tools, teams, and processes. Manual work fills the gaps, and that's a bottleneck we need to solve. You'll design and own the infrastructure that changes that: a single source of truth that powers decisions across Risk, Finance, Product, and Operations.

You'll work closely with our Engineering, Product, Risk, and Finance teams, as well as directly with our leadership, to understand what data they need and make sure they can actually use it.

Your Mission


Build and maintain robust data pipelines across all core systems
Design and implement our company data warehouse from the ground up
Integrate data from internal systems, APIs, and third-party tools
Ensure data reliability, consistency, and accessibility across the company
Enable teams with clean, structured data for faster and better decision-making
Define data ownership, naming conventions, and quality standards that scale


Your Talent


Must-have:
Strong SQL and Python skills - you're genuinely comfortable writing complex queries and automating data workflows
Hands-on experience building and maintaining data pipelines end to end
Experience with data warehouse setup and data modeling
Proven ability to work independently and build systems from scratch
Strong preference:
Experience with modern data stack tools such as dbt, Airflow, Snowflake, or BigQuery
Background in a startup or high-growth environment
Experience collaborating with Product, Risk, or Finance teams
Nice to have:
Experience with BI tools and dashboarding
Basic understanding of financial data or fintech processes
Experience with data quality, testing, and monitoring frameworks
Familiarity with data privacy and security requirements

Our Offer

Growth & Culture

We move fast, we're building a lot from scratch, and we're learning something new every day. You'll be part of a team that doesn't just work, it truly builds something, with a strong sense of ownership and genuine team spirit.

Impact

Your job has a direct impact on the energy transition. Period.

And because this is important to us, we offer extra days off for climate activism.

Flexibility

We work in a hybrid, trust-based environment.

Working from home is the norm for us, two days a week are standard and flexible. We structure the rest in whatever way best supports collaboration and focus within the team.

And if it works for you and your team, you can also work from other locations, workations are possible here.

Perks

You'll receive a flexible bonus that you can use however you like, sports, culture, travel, whatever works for you.

Food & Energy

Long days happen. When the office is running, so is the food: just order something to the office via Wolt.

Team & Vibe

We take our mission seriously, but we don't take ourselves too seriously.

Offsites, team nights, and our "Bees & Beers" are just as much a part of the job as the work itself.

Title: Werkstudent Business Intelligence (m/w/d)
Description:
About MYNE

Wir sind MYNE Homes - der cleverste Weg in die eigenen Traum-Ferienimmobilie. Mit unserem innovativen Co-Ownership Angebot geben wir mehr Menschen die Möglichkeit, sich ihren Traum vom eigenen Ferienhaus zu erfüllen. Gegründet von Serial Entrepreneurs und unterstützt von einigen der renommiertesten Business Angels in Europa (Gründer von Auto1, Smava und Numa) wachsen wir schnell und suchen dich um uns zu unterstützen das nächste Proptech Unicorn aufzubauen!

Als Werkstudent Business Intelligence (m/w/d) unterstützt Du die Super-User unseres Marketingteams und sammelst dabei wertvolle Erfahrungen in einem dynamischen Unternehmensumfeld. Diese Position bietet Dir eine hervorragende Gelegenheit, praxisnahe Fähigkeiten in der Datenanalyse, Business Intelligence und im Bereich Marketing Analytics zu entwickeln.

Deine Aufgaben


Du wirst ab Tag eins vollwertiges Mitglied des Business Intelligence-Teams mit Zugang zum Data Warehouse und allen relevanten Tools.
Du unterstützt die Super-User im Marketingteam bei Datenanalysen, Reportings und wöchentlichen Insights.
Du lernst neue Tools und Technologien kennen und bleibst nah an aktuellen Business-Entwicklungen.
Du übernimmst mit wachsender Erfahrung mehr Verantwortung, z. B. bei Code Reviews.


Dein Profil


Du studierst Wirtschaftswissenschaften, (Wirtschafts-)Informatik oder einen vergleichbaren Studiengang.
Du bist an einer Berliner Hochschule eingeschrieben und hast noch mindestens 12 Monate bis zur Abschlussarbeit.
Du bist 16–20 Stunden pro Woche verfügbar, idealerweise an 2–3 festen Wochentagen.
Du lernst schnell und passt Dich gut an ein dynamisches Umfeld an.
idealerweise beherrschst du G-Sheets und Excel, inklusive Funktionen wie VLOOKUP und Pivot-Tabellen.
Du sprichst fließend Englisch und sehr gut Deutsch.


Kein Muss Aber Von Vorteil


Du hast erste Erfahrungen mit SQL, dbt und/oder version control tools wie Git.
Du kennst Dich mit Performance-Marketing-Kanälen wie Google Ads, Meta o. ä. aus.
Du hast bereits in schnelllebigen Umfeldern wie Startups, Beratung, Investment Banking oder Private Equity Praktika gearbeitet.


Warum wir?


Die Chance eines der führenden Proptech-Startups aktiv mit aufzubauen und zu gestalten.
Du arbeitest direkt mit erfahrenen Gründern und hochkarätigen Investoren aus der Technologie- und Immobilienwelt zusammen.
Verantwortungsvolle und abwechslungsreiche Aufgaben mit Freiraum für eigene Ideen.
Motiviertes und ergebnisorientiertes Team, flache Hierarchien und kurze Entscheidungswege.
Kompetitives Gehalt und die üblichen Startup Benefits.
Und natürlich: Aufenthalte in unseren Traum-Ferienimmobilien!


Über uns

Unsere Mission

Wir sind MYNE Homes - der cleverste Weg in die eigene Traum-Ferienimmobilie. Mit unserem innovativen Co-Ownership Angebot geben wir mehr Menschen die Möglichkeit, sich ihren Traum vom eigenen Ferienhaus zu erfüllen. Gegründet von Serial Entrepreneurs und unterstützt von einigen der renommiertesten Business Angels in Europa (Gründer von Auto1, Smava und Numa), wachsen wir schnell und suchen dich, um gemeinsam das nächste Proptech Unicorn aufzubauen!

Was treibt uns an

Wir wollen mehr Menschen die Möglichkeit geben, sich ihren Traum von der eigenen Ferienimmobilie zu erfüllen.

Title: Sr. Digital Analyst – Tesla Energy EMEA (m/w/d)
Description:
What To Expect
Please submit your CV in English in order to be considered for the role.

Data is deeply embedded in the product, engineering, quality, and customer experience culture at Tesla. This role will collaborate across Demand Generation, Marketing, Web Development, Software, and executive teams to optimize the Web and Digital Customer Experience for Tesla Energy for all of Europe.

This position requires an in-depth understanding of digital marketing best practices, web UI/UX, web traffic data, web marketing channels, first through third party data, and data analysis.

Web Digital Platforms Analyst at Tesla supports campaign planning, site optimization, campaign performance, and data activation.

What You'll Do


Lead web analytics for Tesla Energy in Europe, optimizing customer conversion and interaction
Design Tests and Audit performance in paid media campaigns (Google, Meta, X, and others)
Design and conduct A/B tests on web pages to evaluate performance and drive improvements
Analyze web traffic data to recommend UX enhancements and ensure tagging/tracking compliance
Combine marketing channel data with CRM insights for audience sizing and campaign optimization
Deploy dashboards and conduct impact analysis of business process changes on customer experience
Understand multi-channel marketing campaigns and develop explanatory performance models



What You'll Bring


Relevant enterprise-level experience in web analytics, digital marketing, or data science
Business-level communication skills in German and English (Italian a plus)
Expertise in BigQuery, GA4 funnels, A/B test configuration, and Google Tag Manager
Proficiency in statistical tools (significance tests, regression, time series analysis)
Strong ability to use BI tools (Looker Studio, Tableau, PowerBI) and write SQL
Experience blending web data with CRM/databases across multiple systems
Bonus: Familiarity with UX design, Design Thinking, and data taxonomy (DMP/CDP), Optimizely



, Tesla

Title: Web Analyst (m/f/d) - Marketing & Data
Description:
We are home24, one of the leading home & living e-commerce platforms in continental Europe. We utilize data to provide personalized recommendations, tips, and inspiration for every home. We are looking for a Web Analyst (m/f/d) to join our team. In this role, you will support our marketing teams by ensuring data quality, maintaining our tracking setup, and delivering actionable insights through data visualization.

Your Responsibilities


You drive the implementation and maintenance of tracking concepts in GA4 and sGTM, refining event structures and debugging discrepancies to ensure delivery of high-quality data e.g. for attribution modeling and the design and evaluation of A/B tests
You evaluate the performance of marketing campaigns across various channels
You don't just deliver numbers, but interpret data to help the team understand trends and optimize ROI
You build and maintain interactive dashboards (e.g., in Tableau or Looker) to monitor key marketing metrics, translating business requirements into clear visualizations
You look beyond standard KPIs to investigate the "why" behind the data, conducting ad-hoc deep dives to uncover the root causes of performance shifts and user behavior
You act as a contact person for analytical questions regarding GA4, helping stakeholders understand their data usage needs
You monitor the data flow to ensure accuracy and support the team in identifying and fixing bugs in the tracking landscape


Your Profile


You have professional experience in web analytics, data analysis, or a related field, ideally within an e-commerce environment
You have hands-on experience with Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and (server-side) Google Tag Manager (sGTM)
You are proficient in SQL, capable of merging and transforming large-scale datasets from diverse sources. Proficiency in Python is a strong plus for building scalable data solutions
You have a basic understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to comprehend how tracking is implemented
Your mindset combines natural curiosity for new trends with a sharp eye for detail, ensuring your end-to-end thinking always aligns with the bigger picture
You have excellent English skills and can communicate your findings in a clear, structured way
You thrive in cross-functional collaboration, acting as a bridge between marketing, data, and tech teams


What We Offer You

Your impact


The chance to make an impact in a highly motivated team of professionals
An open-minded culture ready to incorporate new and creative ideas


Your growth


Extensive learning opportunities from our home24 academy and external partners
Room for growth and development within your role through regular feedback rounds


Your path


Hybrid working model: you have the flexibility to work from home on Wednesdays and Fridays in our standard working mode. We are in the office together Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays
The possibility to work up to two months per year within Germany or from an EU country
Up to 30 days paid vacation each year to unwind and recharge
City mobility with a subsidized public transportation ticket and our eco-friendly bike leasing program #ridinghome


Your second home


International campus in the heart of Berlin featuring drive-in bicycle garage, outdoor space and lounge areas, fitness facilities, parent and multi-faith rooms close to the Berlin S-Bahn train station Landsberger Allee
Company benefits including a pension scheme, 30 % off the home24 assortment, and employee discounts at partner organizations
You want to learn more about our home24 company culture and what it is like to be a homie? Follow @teamhome24 on Instagram


If you want to become part of a thrilling success story – we are looking forward to your application. Please send us your application together with your salary expectations and the earliest possible starting date via our career site.

Your home is surely filled with furniture of varying materials and styles. The same is true for home24 as an employer. We celebrate the diversity of the people who work with us. Be who you are and feel welcome to #makeyourselfathome24

You can find all job offers on our career site !

Title: Werkstudent Data Analyst - Reporting Services / Data Engineering & Intelligence (w/m/d)
Description:
Begleite KPMG bei den zukünftigen Herausforderungen unserer Kund:innen. Begeistere auch Du Dich für die Vielfalt unserer Fragestellungen - und mach gemeinsam mit uns den Unterschied. Die KPMG IT Service GmbH ist die IT-Beratung und -Dienstleistung der KPMG AG Wirtschaftsprüfungsgesellschaft. Du verantwortest gemeinsam mit Deinem Team die IT-Infrastruktur und unterstützt die Fachabteilungen bei der Bereitstellung von effizienz- und effektivitätssteigernder Business Software.

Deine Aufgaben

Du denkst nicht in Problemen, sondern gehst lösungsorientiert jede neue Herausforderung an? Dann kannst Du Dich hier einbringen:



Du unterstützt unser Team bei der Entwicklung und Weiterentwicklung moderner Reporting- und Analytics Lösungen im Unternehmensumfeld.
Du analysierst und strukturierst große Datenmengen und bereitest diese adressatengerecht auf.
Du wirkst aktiv in Projekten rund um Microsoft Fabric mit.
Du erstellst aussagekräftige Dashboards und Reports in Microsoft Power BI.
Du unterstützt bei der Weiterentwicklung und Migration bestehender Alteryx Workflows.
Du entwickelst kleinere Datenpipelines und Automatisierungen, z. B. mit Python.
Du führst Qualitätssicherungen und Tests von Reports sowie Datenmodellen durch.



Dein Profil



Du befindest Dich in einem laufenden Studium der (Wirtschafts-) Informatik, Data Science oder eines vergleichbaren Studiengangs.
Du bringst erste praktische Erfahrungen im Bereich Datenanalyse, Reporting oder Business Intelligence mit.
Idealerweise hast Du bereits Berührungspunkte mit Power BI, Python, SQL oder Alteryx.
Du interessierst Dich für moderne Data Plattformen und Technologien wie Microsoft Fabric.
Du verfügst über ein ausgeprägtes analytisches Denkvermögen sowie eine strukturierte und eigenständige Arbeitsweise.
Du arbeitest gerne im Team und überzeugst durch eine hohe Lernbereitschaft.



Deine Benefits



Willkommen im Team Damit Du von Beginn an voll bei uns durchstarten kannst, begleiten wir Dich mit strukturierten Onboarding-Maßnahmen während der gesamten Orientierungsphase. Dabei steht Dir immer eine persönliche Ansprechperson zur Seite.
Flexibel & erfolgreich Bei uns kannst Du Studium und erste Praxiserfahrung bequem vereinbaren. Dabei erhältst Du intensive Einblicke in die Arbeitswelt bei KPMG. In Absprache mit Deinem Team kannst Du Deine Arbeitszeit flexibel gestalten. So kommt Dein Studium nicht zu kurz.
Feedback & Entwicklung Vom ersten Tag an bist Du fester Bestandteil des Teams und übernimmst Verantwortung in herausfordernden (Teil-) Projekten. Mit regelmäßigem Feedback fördern wir zudem Deine persönliche und fachliche Weiterentwicklung.
Wertvolles Karrierenetzwerk Bei unseren YOUnior .Network-Aktivitäten stehen Vernetzung und der gemeinsame Austausch zwischen Auszubildenden, Dual Studierenden, Praktikanten/Praktikantinnen und Werkstudenten/Werkstudentinnen im Fokus.
Die abgebildeten Leistungen können je nach Position und Standort leicht variieren.
Show more

Title: Reporting Process & Analytics Expert EQS (all genders), befristet bis 31.03.2027
Description:
CDO Expert Analysis & Reporting (all genders), befristet bis zum 31.03.2027, HQ-Berlin

Ohne uns gibt’s weder Coke, Fanta noch Sprite im Supermarktregal. Klingt verrückt, ist aber so.

Denn wir sind Coca‑Cola Europacific Partners Deutschland (CCEP) und verantworten bundesweit die Abfüllung und den Vertrieb von Coca‑Cola Markenprodukten.

Und damit das auch weiterhin so reibungslos funktioniert, brauchen wir Menschen wie dich im Bereich Cold Drink Equipment! Unser Team soll nicht nur größer, sondern auch bunter werden. Es ist also egal, woher du kommst, woran du glaubst oder welches Geschlecht du hast. Bei uns zählt einzig und allein deine Persönlichkeit und deine Motivation.

Davon hast du mehr als genug? Dann werde Teil von Deutschlands größtem Getränkehersteller und gestalte mit uns datenbasierte Entscheidungen für die Zukunft.

Was wir dir bieten

Planungssicherheit:

Mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten und verlässlicher Planung kannst du Beruf und Familie problemlos miteinander vereinbaren. So behältst du nicht nur deinen Job im Blick, sondern auch dein Privatleben.

Persönliche Weiterentwicklung:

Wir fordern dich nicht nur, sondern fördern dich auch. Mit einer Vielzahl an Netzwerkevents und Förderprogrammen, wie z.B. „Cross-Mentoring“ unterstützen wir deine individuelle Entwicklung. Bei uns steht deine Individualität im Mittelpunkt.      

Aktionär werden:

Wir belohnen deine Investition in unser Unternehmen, indem du für jede Aktie, die du kaufst und 12 Monate lang behältst, kostenlos eine Zusatzaktie von uns bekommst.

Dein Profil

Wir suchen Menschen, keine Nummern. Es ist also egal, ob dein Lebenslauf lückenlos ist oder nicht. Bei uns zählen Persönlichkeit und Motivation. Also keine Sorge, wenn du nicht alle Anforderungen mitbringst.


Mehrjährige Erfahrung im Bereich Analyse, Reporting oder Controlling
Abgeschlossenes Studium oder vergleichbare Qualifikation mit starkem Zahlenverständnis
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in Excel sowie idealerweise Power BI, SAP oder ähnlichen Tools
Erfahrung in Projektmanagement, Prozessoptimierung und datenbasierter Steuerung


Deine Aufgaben


Erstellung und Analyse von CDO‑Reports sowie Ableitung von Handlungsempfehlungen für das Business
Durchführung von Soll‑Ist‑Vergleichen und KPI‑Tracking zur Steuerung der Performance
Identifikation und Umsetzung digitaler Lösungen zur Effizienzsteigerung im Equipment‑Umfeld
Steuerung und Umsetzung nationaler und internationaler Projekte mit Fokus auf Kostenoptimierung und Prozessverbesserung


Was du sonst noch wissen solltest:

Wir möchten die Stelle gerne schnellstmöglich besetzten. Sie erfordert eine Büro-Präsenz von drei Tagen pro Woche in Berlin und zudem eine Reisebereitschaft von bis zu 50 %

Title: NGS Data Analyst for Molecular Oncology
Description:
Alacris Theranostics GmbH is a leader in innovative molecular diagnostics, specialising in personalised oncology. Our cutting-edge solutions combine next-generation sequencing (NGS), advanced bioinformatics, and systems biology to transform scientific insights into enhanced cancer diagnostics and more precise treatment decisions. We collaborate closely with leading research institutions, hospitals, and pharmaceutical partners to drive meaningful and lasting improvements in patient outcomes.


Tasks


We are seeking an experienced Bioinformatician / Computational Biologist with a strong track record in next-generation sequencing (NGS) data analysis. The successful candidate will be responsible for processing and analysing NGS datasets - including RNA-Seq, whole-exome sequencing (WES), whole-genome sequencing (WGS), and single-cell sequencing - derived from tumour samples.


Requirements


The ideal candidate will demonstrate excellent programming skills in R and Python, along with proven expertise in NGS data analysis and a solid foundation in human molecular genetics. Knowledge of statistics, high-dimensional data analysis, and cancer genomics is highly desirable.




We are looking for a candidate with a genuine interest in precision oncology, a proactive approach to learning, and a commitment to developing new skills. Strong motivation, a collaborative mindset, and excellent communication skills are essential


Benefits


You will join a multidisciplinary team of bioinformaticians, molecular biologists, and software engineers dedicated to developing innovative analysis pipelines and diagnostic solutions in oncology.




We offer a rewarding and challenging role within a dynamic biotech company based in the vibrant city of Berlin. The position comes with a competitive salary and a stimulating working environment. The appointment is available immediately and will initially be for a two-year term, with the possibility of extension.




How to Apply? Interested candidates should provide a CV, publication list, details of previous research and work experience, and contact details of three referees as a single PDF file, citing reference ALA-NGSA-0426.




The closing date for applications is April 15, 2026.

Title: Data Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
About us

At Venatic Intelligence, we build software for complex data analysis, focusing on the challenge of making sense of massive, fragmented, and unstructured datasets. To address this, we develop applications that integrate disparate data sources, resolve complex entities, and map hidden networks.




We are looking for a Data Engineer (m/w/d) to join our team in Berlin. You will play a central role in expanding our core architecture, connecting new data streams, and building the analytical tools that power our applications.




What you'll do

Design and build robust data pipelines that extract, clean, and load complex datasets from highly diverse sources.
Engineer custom data acquisition tools that rely on proxy-routed networks to securely navigate and extract information from complex digital environments.
Manage and scale our core database architecture. You will work with graph databases (e.g., Neo4j) for entity resolution and search indices (e.g., Elasticsearch) for rapid retrieval.
Develop resilient APIs and backend services to seamlessly integrate new capabilities into our platform.
Build and refine specialized analytical dashboards and UI components using Vue.js/Nuxt 3, allowing users to interact directly with complex data models.
Deploy, optimize, and secure our infrastructure across various hyperscalers (e.g., AWS, GCP, OCI).
Take technical ownership of your projects, from initial architecture decisions to deployment, using standard workflows (e.g., GitHub, Jira, Confluence).




What we're looking for

Roughly 2 years of hands-on experience in software engineering, data engineering, or backend development.
Strong programming skills in Node.js, TypeScript, and Python.
Demonstrable experience in building data pipelines, handling unstructured data, or developing specialized data extraction tools.
Solid understanding of data modeling. You should know your way around NoSQL, graph (e.g., Neo4j), and search (e.g., Elasticsearch) architectures.
Practical frontend experience, ideally with Vue.js/Nuxt 3, to build intuitive interfaces for complex data.
Comfortable managing cloud environments (e.g., GCP, OCI, AWS) and navigating modern engineering toolchains.
You are a pragmatic problem-solver. You care about building secure, highly performant software that works reliably in the real world.
Fluent English. Proficiency in German is a plus, but not required.




What we offer

A permanent role in a fast-growing, technology-driven firm.
The opportunity to build high-impact software and tackle complex architectural challenges.
Close mentorship within a small team of highly skilled engineers and analysts.
Competitive compensation.
28 days of annual leave.
Flexible working hours with the option to work remotely.
A tailored benefits package that fits your personal needs.




To apply, please send your CV and a brief introduction to hr@venatic-intelligence.com.




Venatic Intelligence is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, and we believe that a range of perspectives, experiences, and languages makes our work sharper and our analysis stronger. We hire on the basis of merit and are committed to equal opportunity for all candidates, regardless of background, identity, or circumstance.

  • Engineer custom data acquisition tools that rely on proxy-routed networks to securely navigate and extract information from complex digital environments.
  • Manage and scale our core database architecture. You will work with graph databases (e.g., Neo4j) for entity resolution and search indices (e.g., Elasticsearch) for rapid retrieval.
  • Develop resilient APIs and backend services to seamlessly integrate new capabilities into our platform.
  • Build and refine specialized analytical dashboards and UI components using Vue.js/Nuxt 3, allowing users to interact directly with complex data models.
  • Deploy, optimize, and secure our infrastructure across various hyperscalers (e.g., AWS, GCP, OCI).
  • Take technical ownership of your projects, from initial architecture decisions to deployment, using standard workflows (e.g., GitHub, Jira, Confluence).


What we're looking for

  • Roughly 2 years of hands-on experience in software engineering, data engineering, or backend development.
  • Strong programming skills in Node.js, TypeScript, and Python.
  • Demonstrable experience in building data pipelines, handling unstructured data, or developing specialized data extraction tools.
  • Solid understanding of data modeling. You should know your way around NoSQL, graph (e.g., Neo4j), and search (e.g., Elasticsearch) architectures.
  • Practical frontend experience, ideally with Vue.js/Nuxt 3, to build intuitive interfaces for complex data.
  • Comfortable managing cloud environments (e.g., GCP, OCI, AWS) and navigating modern engineering toolchains.
  • You are a pragmatic problem-solver. You care about building secure, highly performant software that works reliably in the real world.
  • Fluent English. Proficiency in German is a plus, but not required.


What we offer

  • A permanent role in a fast-growing, technology-driven firm.
  • The opportunity to build high-impact software and tackle complex architectural challenges.
  • Close mentorship within a small team of highly skilled engineers and analysts.
  • Competitive compensation.
  • 28 days of annual leave.
  • Flexible working hours with the option to work remotely.
  • A tailored benefits package that fits your personal needs.


To apply, please send your CV and a brief introduction to hr@venatic-intelligence.com.


Venatic Intelligence is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, and we believe that a range of perspectives, experiences, and languages makes our work sharper and our analysis stronger. We hire on the basis of merit and are committed to equal opportunity for all candidates, regardless of background, identity, or circumstance.

Title: Data Scientist / Data Analyst (w/m/d)
Description:
Wachstum braucht Freiheit.

Wir sind eine unabhängige IT-Management- und IT-Security-Beratung, die dort ansetzt, wo Digitalisierung anspruchsvoll wird: bei Informationssicherheit, IT-Services und -Betrieb, sowie organisatorischer Transformation. Wir unterstützen unsere Kunden dabei, komplexe Herausforderungen zu meistern – individuell, strukturiert und mit einem klaren Blick für das Wesentliche.

Was uns antreibt ist der Anspruch, nachhaltige Lösungen zu schaffen, die wirklich wirken. Dafür verbinden wir tiefgehende fachliche Expertise mit methodischem Know-how und einem partnerschaftlichen Beratungsansatz. Unsere Projekte sind vielfältig, verantwortungsvoll und gesellschaftlich relevant – vom Mittelstand bis zu großen Organisationen und öffentlichen Auftraggebern.

Mindestens genauso wichtig wie unsere Projekte sind die Menschen bei HiSolutions. Wir glauben an Eigenverantwortung, Vertrauen und kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung. Bei uns arbeitest Du in engagierten Teams, mit ausgeprägtem Pioniergeist. Wir fördern fachliches Wachstum ebenso wie persönliche Entwicklung und schaffen ein Arbeitsumfeld, in dem Offenheit, Qualität und gegenseitige Wertschätzung selbstverständlich sind.

Freue Dich auf spannende Aufgaben.


Erhebe komplexe Datenmengen bei unseren Kunden, von vorhandenen Datenquellen (z. B. Inventar/Discovery, Lizenzdaten, Verträge, Procurement, Finance o.ä.)
Bewerte die Daten hinsichtlich Qualität, Vollständigkeit, Konsistenz und Aussagekraft und dokumentiere Datenmodelle sowie Datenflüsse
Führe Datenanalysen durch (z. B. Nutzung vs. Bereitstellung, Lizenz- und Kostenauffälligkeiten, Hersteller-/Produkt-Abhängigkeiten, Shadow IT-Indikatoren) und leite Erkenntnisse ab, die die digitale Souveränität unterstützen (z. B. Konzentrationsrisiken, Lock-in-Indikatoren, Vertrags-/Nutzungsrisiken, Datenzugänglichkeit)
Bereite Ergebnisse so auf, dass Entscheider sie verstehen: Dashboards, Reports oder Analyse-Berichte


Damit überzeugst Du uns.


Abgeschlossenes Studium sowie 1-3 Jahre Berufserfahrung im Bereich Data Science oder Datenanalyse
Gute Kenntnisse in Datenanalyse-, Reporting- und Visualisierungstools
Analytisches Verständnis, um Komplexität zu strukturieren und Zusammenhänge zu verdeutlichen, sowie hohe Eigenmotivation und gewissenhafte Arbeitsweise
Sehr gute Präsentations- und Kommunikationsfähigkeiten (Deutsch verhandlungssicher)


Die Liste der Anforderungen ist eine Wunschliste. Bitte bewirb Dich auch dann, wenn Du nicht alle Punkte erfüllst, vieles kannst Du während der Arbeit bei uns lernen.

Darüber hinaus haben wir noch viel zu bieten.


Grün unterwegs: Wir bezuschussen nicht nur Dein Jobrad, sondern auch Dein ÖPNV-Ticket, sodass die Umwelt und Dein Geldbeutel geschont wird.
In Bewegung bleiben: Wir fördern Deine Fitness-Mitgliedschaft und Deine Gesundheitsvorsorge.
Maximale Individualität: Wir ermöglichen die Vereinbarkeit von Familie und Job nach Deinen Bedürfnissen, mit der Option des Mobilen Arbeitens.
Zeit für Erholung: Wir bieten Dir 30 Tage Urlaub im Jahr. Nimm Dir mit nur einem Tag an Heiligabend und Silvester frei. Bei einer länger gewünschten Auszeit ermöglichen wir Dir ein Sabbatical.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
As a Data Engineer at PERGOLUX, you will build and own the data pipelines that enable fast, reliable operational insights across the business. Your work ensures the company can trust its data, automate confidently, and make high-impact decisions in real time. You will join a lean, senior data team where reliability, clarity, and business value—not technical complexity—define success.

At PERGOLUX, we combine Scandinavian-inspired design with a collaborative, ambitious culture grounded in integrity, innovation, and unity.

What You’ll Do


Build and maintain a dependable ingestion and delivery layer that moves data into Snowflake and feeds operational tools with high accuracy and low maintenance overhead.
Own the ingestion backbone across systems such as HubSpot, Klaviyo, Google Ads, and Canary7, selecting the tools and approaches that maximize stability and simplicity.
Design monitoring, observability, and alerting that eliminate silent failures and give stakeholders full visibility into data health and freshness.
Define pragmatic orchestration patterns that ensure scalable, cost-efficient, and resilient pipelines—introducing new tooling only when it clearly reduces failure modes or maintenance costs.
Lead simplification initiatives within the first 90 days by improving stability, reducing pipeline breaks, and implementing a transparent, well-instrumented data platform.


What You’ll Bring


A pragmatic mindset focused on simplicity, reliability, and business value over unnecessary technical complexity.
Strong systems thinking and the ability to evaluate downstream impact when designing pipelines and data flows.
Fluency in Python, SQL, and Git, with a deep understanding of production-grade data engineering concepts such as data contracts, schema evolution, idempotency, and failure-state management.
Ability to create force-multiplying infrastructure that removes ambiguity, eliminates manual work, and accelerates Analytics Engineering workflows.
A disciplined approach to protecting platform stability, reducing technical debt, and ensuring predictable, trustworthy data delivery.


Benefits


Competitive Salary & Bonus Structure – Your success is rewarded with strong compensation and performance-based bonuses.
Real Ownership – Take meaningful responsibility in your role and directly influence key business outcomes.
International Team – Collaborate with passionate colleagues across Europe, gaining exposure to diverse perspectives and ideas.
Outdoor Lifestyle Discounts – Enjoy exclusive discounts on PERGOLUX products.
Fast-Track Career Opportunities – As we scale quickly, you’ll have significant opportunities to grow into senior leadership roles.
Team Culture – Be part of regular team events, retreats, and a culture that celebrates wins—big and small.
Bike Leasing Program – Benefit from a sustainable and cost-effective bike leasing option to support commuting and an active lifestyle.


How to Apply

Ready to shape the data foundation of a fast-scaling global brand? Apply through our career channels:

PERGOLUX LinkedIn

PERGOLUX Career Page

Description:
Berlin
Vollzeit
sofort
IT
Berufserfahrene


Als Berliner Traditionsunternehmen gehört die Iden Gruppe zu den führenden Handelshäusern für Schreib- und Spielwaren sowie Festbedarf. Die Geschäftsfelder sind vielfältig und zukunftsorientiert. Mit seinen Kernkompetenzen agiert das Handelshaus als Kooperationspartner des stationären Handels, Systemdienstleister der Filialisten und zuverlässiger Dienstleister der führenden E-Commerce-Plattformen. Die Grundlagen des Erfolges liegen in der kontinuierlichen Entwicklung des Unternehmens auf dem Fundament seiner Werte Tradition, Unabhängigkeit und Dynamik. Mit ihren vier leistungsstarken Standorten bietet die Iden Gruppe eine sichere Basis für die vielfältigen Transformationsprozesse der Zukunft.

Ab sofort suchen wir Sie in Vollzeit als Data Engineer (m/w/d) für unsere Zentrale am Berliner Südkreuz.

Was Sie Erwartet


Entwicklung, Betreuung und Optimierung von ETL Prozessen
Aufbau und Weiterentwicklung unseres Datalakes sowie der zugrunde liegenden Datenarchitektur
Anbindung und Integration verschiedener Datenquellen (z. B. ERP-, CRM-, Shop-, Logistik- oder Dritt-Systeme)
Erstellung und Optimierung komplexer SQL-Abfragen, Views und Datenmodelle
Sicherstellung von Datenqualität, Datenkonsistenz und Performance
Bereitstellung strukturierter Datenmodelle für nachgelagerte Reporting- und BI-Lösungen, insbesondere Power BI
Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen zur Analyse von Anforderungen und Umsetzung datengetriebener Lösungen
Dokumentation von Datenflüssen, Prozessen und Strukturen


Was Sie Mitbringen Sollten


Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science oder vergleichbare Qualifikation
Erfahrung im Bereich Data Engineering, Datenbanken und Business Intelligence
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in SQL und relationalen Datenbanken
Erfahrung mit Datalake- oder Data-Warehouse-Strukturen
Verständnis für Datenmodellierung und Datenintegration
Idealerweise Erfahrung mit Business Central und Power BI
Verständnis für betriebswirtschaftliche Prozesse
Strukturierte, analytische und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise, sowie ausgeprägte Teamfähigkeit
Deutschkenntnisse auf Niveau C1


Was Wir Bieten

Neben einem sicheren Arbeitsplatz in einem dynamischen, zukunftsorientierten Unternehmen mit Tradition bieten wir Ihnen:

Präsenztätigkeit in Gleitzeit mit Kernzeit

30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr

Steuerfreie Benefit-Karte bis zu 50 € mtl.

Jubiläumsgratifikationen

Urlaubsgeld

Bezuschusstes Deutschlandticket für 40 €
