Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About CuspAI

CuspAI is the frontier AI company on a mission to solve the breakthrough materials needed to power human progress. While nature took billions of years to perfect molecules, we are harnessing AI to unlock trillion-dollar materials breakthroughs in months, not millennia. Our founding team is the most cited in the world, comprised of world-class researchers in AI, chemistry and engineering.

We are working on some of the hardest and most important challenges including energy, clean water, the future of compute, and carbon capture, and this is just the start of what our 'search engine' for next-generation materials will unlock.

We invite you to be part of a diverse, innovative team at the intersection of AI and materials science, working to create impactful partnerships that drive innovation, scalability, and industry collaboration. This work matters. Your work matters.

We’re on the cusp of the on-demand materials era. Join us.

The Role

As we grow, we are seeking a Data Engineer to play a crucial part in driving our research and development efforts forward.

Your Impact

As a Data Engineer you will be part of the new team building the infrastructure that underpins and acts as the critical bridge between raw chemical data and our machine learning models.

Your main focus will be to build the pipeline infrastructure and tooling for data ingestion, moving towards self-serve setup for the scientific team members. You'll also be responsible for securing, collecting, cleaning, standardising, and tagging diverse chemical datasets to create high-quality training data for our ML researchers while working closely with our chemistry team to ensure scientific accuracy.

What You Will Do

Data Pipeline Development


Design and build robust data pipelines for materials science datasets, experimental results, and computational chemistry outputs.
Develop processes to integrate diverse data sources including materials databases, literature, patent filings, and laboratory instruments.
Create automated workflows for processing crystallographic data, molecular structures, and materials properties (you don’t need to have direct domain experience - we can help bring you up to speed!).
Build scalable systems to handle high-throughput computational chemistry calculations and experimental data.


Data Quality & Standardisation


Partner closely with the scientific and research teams to implement automated quality checks for crystal structure data, chemical compositions, and experimental measurements.
Create standardisation protocols for materials nomenclature, units, and measurement conditions.
Build monitoring systems to ensure data integrity across all pipelines.


Collaboration & Integration


You will also be working hand in hand with ML researchers to understand data requirements for model training and inference.
Partner with materials scientists to ensure accurate representation of domain knowledge in data schemas.
Integrate with laboratory automation systems and computational chemistry software.
Support real-time data needs for AI-driven materials discovery experiments.


Must Have Skills and Qualifications


You are someone who gets excited about the opportunity to enable scientists to work on world changing challenges in this domain, with a personal interest in the potential applications of the technology that Cusp is building.
You’re a builder of tools and infrastructure who enjoys making life as easy as possible for the teams, providing self-serve, reliable and scalable ingestion pipelines.
You have at least 3+ years experience in data engineering roles, preferably in scientific or research environments - you would be joining as a data engineering subject matter expert who can not only work autonomously but also provide guidance on best practice.
High level of proficiency in Python and databases with experience in large-scale data processing - as part of our engineering team you’ll be programming regularly, not just scripting.
You’re an advanced user of workflow orchestration tools (e.g. Airflow, Prefect, Dagster, Flyte or similar).
Solid experience with containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes) and CI/CD practices.
You have direct experience handling large/complex datasets and are interested in working with scientific packages.
You’re a fast learner when it comes to new tools/systems.
You enjoy (and have experience in) designing systems that scale with growing data volumes and user demands.
Understanding and appreciation of DevOps practices is also important.


Bonus Points (But Not Critical)


You’ve worked with data from scientific computing (simulations or experiments).
Knowledge of machine learning data requirements and MLOps practices, including pre-processing/processing as part of model training.
An academic background in Materials Science, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or related field.


Even more bonus points if you have an understanding of crystallography, materials properties, and computational chemistry concepts!

What we offer


A competitive salary: We value and reward impact and growth
Equity in CuspAI: You have a stake in the success of the company
Time off to stay fresh: 28 days holiday (DE, NL, UK) or 21 days holiday (JP, SG, US), in addition to local public holidays
‘Gold Standard’ parental leave: 26 weeks (primary caregiver) and 12 weeks (secondary caregiver) at full pay - we look after you and your family while we work on the most important materials discovery problems together
Professional development budget: We invest in your career development so you can stay up to date with the latest industry knowledge or add to your skills to increase impact and growth
Solve meaningful problems: See how your work has a direct impact on advancing materials science and solving sustainability and climate-related problems through the creation and application of bleeding-edge SOTA technology and revolutionary techniques
True interdisciplinary teamwork: Be part of a deeply collaborative environment bridging AI research, computational chemistry, and experimental science - work with world-class researchers and engineers who enjoy sharing knowledge and supporting each other


Join us in shaping the future of materials with AI. Together, we can create groundbreaking solutions for a more sustainable world.

CuspAI is an equal opportunities employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We do not discriminate on the basis of sex, race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), veteran status, or any other basis protected by applicable law.

We actively encourage applications from all backgrounds and value the unique perspectives and contributions that diversity brings to our team.

Please let us know If you require any specific adjustments during or after the interview process. We will do everything we can within reason to accommodate.

About CuspAI

CuspAI is the frontier AI company on a mission to solve the breakthrough materials needed to power human progress. While nature took billions of years to perfect molecules, we are harnessing AI to unlock trillion-dollar materials breakthroughs in months, not millennia. Our founding team is the most cited in the world, comprised of world-class researchers in AI, chemistry and engineering.

We are working on some of the hardest and most important challenges including energy, clean water, the future of compute, and carbon capture, and this is just the start of what our 'search engine' for next-generation materials will unlock.

We invite you to be part of a diverse, innovative team at the intersection of AI and materials science, working to create impactful partnerships that drive innovation, scalability, and industry collaboration. This work matters. Your work matters.

We’re on the cusp of the on-demand materials era. Join us.

The Role

As we grow, we are seeking a Data Engineer to play a crucial part in driving our research and development efforts forward.

Your Impact

As a Data Engineer you will be part of the new team building the infrastructure that underpins and acts as the critical bridge between raw chemical data and our machine learning models.

Your main focus will be to build the pipeline infrastructure and tooling for data ingestion, moving towards self-serve setup for the scientific team members. You'll also be responsible for securing, collecting, cleaning, standardising, and tagging diverse chemical datasets to create high-quality training data for our ML researchers while working closely with our chemistry team to ensure scientific accuracy.

What You Will Do

Data Pipeline Development

  • Design and build robust data pipelines for materials science datasets, experimental results, and computational chemistry outputs.
  • Develop processes to integrate diverse data sources including materials databases, literature, patent filings, and laboratory instruments.
  • Create automated workflows for processing crystallographic data, molecular structures, and materials properties (you don’t need to have direct domain experience - we can help bring you up to speed!).
  • Build scalable systems to handle high-throughput computational chemistry calculations and experimental data.

Data Quality & Standardisation

  • Partner closely with the scientific and research teams to implement automated quality checks for crystal structure data, chemical compositions, and experimental measurements.
  • Create standardisation protocols for materials nomenclature, units, and measurement conditions.
  • Build monitoring systems to ensure data integrity across all pipelines.

Collaboration & Integration

  • An academic background in Materials Science, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or related field.

Even more bonus points if you have an understanding of crystallography, materials properties, and computational chemistry concepts!

Title: Werkstudent*in Data & AI in Commercial Effectiveness
Description:
Für unseren Standort im Herzen von BERLIN (Nähe Park Gleisdreieck) suchen wir ab dem nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt Unterstützung (15-20h/Woche) im Team Commercial Effectiveness.

Über uns

Als Werkstudent im Bereich CFC Commercial Effectiveness unterstützt du unser Team dabei, Vertriebsstrategien zu optimieren, Kundensegmentierungen weiterzuentwickeln und datenbasierte Entscheidungen zu fördern. Du arbeitest direkt an praxisrelevanten Projekten – mit einem besonderen Fokus auf datengetriebene Analysen– und erhältst tiefe Einblicke in die kommerzielle Exzellenz eines globalen Pharmaunternehmens.

Dein Profil


Du bist immatrikuliert in einem Studium wie z. B.: BWL, Business Administration, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Gesundheitsökonomie oder ähnliches.
Du sprichst Deutsch auf C1/C2 Niveau und bringst gute Englischkenntnisse (B2 oder höher) mit.
Du denkst analytisch und gehst strukturiert an Aufgaben heran.
Du arbeitest eigenständig und eignest dir schnell neue Themen an.
Du arbeitest gerne im Team und kommunizierst klar und auf den Punkt.
Du arbeitest zuverlässig und mit einem hohen Qualitätsanspruch.
Du kannst komplexe Inhalte einfach und verständlich erklären.


Folgende Interessen Oder Kenntnisse Bringst Du Mit

Datenanalyse & Tools


Sehr gute Kenntnisse in MS Office (insb. Excel, PowerPoint, PowerQuery)
Erste Erfahrung mit BI-Tools (z. B. Power BI, Tableau)
Grundkenntnisse in SQL
Erste Erfahrung mit Datenbanken (z. B. Snowflake) von Vorteil
Interesse an pharmazeutischen Märkten und Vertriebsprozessen


Künstliche Intelligenz (AI)


Interesse an Large Language Models (LLMs) im Business-Kontext
Erfahrung mit Tools wie ChatGPT, Copilot oder Claude
Grundkenntnisse in Prompt Engineering und AI Use Cases


Prozessautomatisierung


Erste Erfahrung mit Microsoft Power Automate oder ähnlichen Tools von Vorteil
Verständnis für die Automatisierung von Geschäftsprozessen


Deine Aufgaben

Datenanalyse & Reporting


Analyse von Vertriebs- und Marktdaten sowie Datenextraktion mittels SQL
Erstellung von Reports und Dashboards zur Steuerung der Commercial Effectiveness
Identifikation von Trends und Ableitung konkreter Handlungsempfehlungen


AI & LLM-Anwendungen


Evaluierung und Einsatz von LLM-basierten Tools (z. B. ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude) für Business Use Cases
Unterstützung bei der Entwicklung von Prompt-Strategien und AI-Anwendungsfällen im Vertrieb
Mitarbeit an Prototypen und Proof-of-Concepts für datengetriebene Lösungen


Prozessautomatisierung


Unterstützung bei der Automatisierung von Geschäftsprozessen mit Microsoft Power Automate
Identifikation von Automatisierungspotenzialen und Entwicklung von Workflows


Commercial & Sales Effectiveness


Analyse und Optimierung von Vertriebsprozessen sowie KPI-Auswertungen
Unterstützung bei Territory-Alignment-Projekten
Erstellung von Präsentationen und Entscheidungsvorlagen


Wir bieten dir


Flexible Arbeitszeiten und die Möglichkeit zum teilweisen mobilen Arbeiten
Ein modernes, internationales Arbeitsumfeld
Zugang zu Sanofi-Lernplattformen und internen Trainings
Eine individuelle und strukturierte Einarbeitung in Deine Aufgaben sowie Mitarbeit an strategisch relevanten Projekten


Wir freuen uns auf die Bewerbung. Bitte richte diese ausschließlich elektronisch an unseren Partner Provadis Professionals GmbH unter Werkstudent*in Data & AI in Commercial Effectiveness

Title: Werkstudent (w/m/d) Data Science
Description:
Standort: BLN H

Art der Anstellung: Fixed Term / Part-time

Momentaufnahme von deinem Tag

Du bist ein engagierter und verlässlicher Student im Bereich Informatik, Data Science oder einem verwandten Studiengang und möchtest dein theoretisches Wissen aus den Bereichen Künstliche Intelligenz (KI), Datenanalyse und Programmierung in die Praxis umsetzen? Du möchtest unser Team dabei unterstützen, innovative AI-Anwendungen in unserer global agierenden Marketingabteilung weiterzuentwickeln, Daten aufzubereiten und zu analysieren sowie spannende Herausforderungen in einem dynamischen Umfeld zu meistern? Dann bist du bei uns genau richtig!

Wie du etwas bewirken kannst


Unterstützung bei der Datenaufbereitung, -analyse und -visualisierung, um die Performance unserer AI-Algorithmen zu optimieren
Testing und Validierung der bestehenden AI-Modelle sowie kontinuierliche Verbesserung der Anwendungen
Zusammenarbeit mit interdisziplinären Teams zur Integration der AI-Lösungen in bestehende Systeme und Marketingprozesse
Dokumentation und Visualisierung von Datenstrukturen, Prozessen und bestehenden IT-Systemen



Was du mitbringst


Derzeitiges Studium im Bereich Informatik, Data Science oder eines vergleichbaren technischen Studiengangs, idealerweise mit Schwerpunkt Künstliche Intelligenz und Programmierung
Interesse an Marketing sowie an der Anwendung technologischer Lösungen zur Optimierung von Marketingprozessen
Kenntnisse in Python, SQL sowie Data-Science-Bibliotheken wie Pandas, NumPy oder Scikit-learn
Kenntnisse in Datenanalyse, Machine-Learning-Konzepten und technischer Dokumentation
Selbstständige Arbeitsweise, schnelle Auffassungsgabe sowie Begeisterung für innovative Technologien
Teamfähigkeit, Kommunikationsstärke sowie sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift



Über das Team

Unser Geschäftsbereich Gas Services bietet emissionsarme Stromerzeugung durch Service und Dekarbonisierung. Emissionsfreie oder emissionsarme Stromerzeugung und alle Gasturbinen unter einem Dach, Dampfturbinen und Generatoren. Dekarbonisierungschancen durch Serviceangebote, Modernisierung und Digitalisierung der Flotte.

Wer ist Siemens Energy?

Wir bei Siemens Energy sind mehr als nur ein Energietechnologieunternehmen. Mit ~100,000 engagierten Mitarbeitern in mehr als 90 Ländern entwickeln wir die Energiesysteme der Zukunft und stellen sicher, dass der wachsende Energiebedarf der Weltgemeinschaft zuverlässig und nachhaltig gedeckt wird. Die in unseren Forschungsabteilungen und Fabriken entstehenden Technologien treiben die Energiewende voran und bilden die Grundlage für ein Sechstel der weltweiten Stromerzeugung.

Unser globales Team setzt sich dafür ein, nachhaltige, zuverlässige und bezahlbare Energie Wirklichkeit werden zu lassen, indem es die Grenzen des Möglichen immer weiter hinausschiebt. Wir blicken zurück auf eine 150-jährige Tradition der Innovation, die unsere Suche nach Mitarbeitern bestärkt, die unseren Fokus auf Dekarbonisierung, neue Technologien und Energiewandel unterstützen.

Erfahre mehr darüber, wie du bei Siemens Energy etwas bewegen kannst: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

Unser Engagement für Vielfalt

Gut, dass wir nicht alle gleich sind. Vielfalt macht uns leistungsstark. Wir leben von Inklusion und unsere gemeinsame kreative Energie wird von über 130 Nationalitäten gespeist. Siemens Energy setzt auf den Charakter - unabhängig von ethnischem Hintergrund, Geschlecht, Alter, Religion, Identität oder Behinderung. Wir versorgen die Gesellschaft mit Energie - die ganze Gesellschaft - und wir diskriminieren nicht aufgrund von Unterschieden, die zwischen uns bestehen.

Benefits


Spannende Einblicke in ein internationales Unternehmen
Lege mit uns den Grundstein für deine Karriere



Wir legen Wert auf Chancengleichheit und freuen uns über Bewerbungen von Menschen mit Behinderung.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Company overview:

Blue Orange Digital is a boutique data & AI consultancy that delivers enterprise-grade results. We design and build modern data platforms, analytics, and ML/AI Agent solutions for mid‑market and enterprise clients across Private Equity, Financial Services, Healthcare, and Retail.

Our teams work with technologies like Databricks, Snowflake, dbt, and the broader Microsoft ecosystem to turn messy, real-world data into trustworthy, actionable insight.

We’re a builder‑led, client‑first culture that prizes ownership, clear communication, and shipping high‑impact work.

Note: Please submit your resume in English, as all application materials must be in English for review and consideration.

Position Overview

Blue Orange Digital is seeking a Mid‑Level Data Engineer to join a 26‑week enterprise data warehouse implementation for a global commodities trading and supply chain company, embedded full‑time from Week 3 through Week 22.

You will build and maintain Bronze and Silver layer pipelines on Microsoft Fabric, implementing ingestion from multi‑source ERP and CTRM systems and transformations that feed Gold‑layer data products across trading, risk, and finance domains.

Working within a project‑based delivery team alongside a Senior Data Engineer and Solutions Architect, you will contribute to a structured, phase‑gated delivery with clear milestones and real production output.

Responsibilities


Implement Bronze ingestion pipelines from source systems including CTRM instances (Irely) and ERP/SAP S/4HANA feeds using Microsoft Fabric Data Factory
Build Silver‑layer transformation logic in Fabric notebooks using PySpark and SQL on Delta Lake tables, covering cleansing, standardization, and business rule application
Support development of Gold‑layer outputs aligned to Trader P&L, Global Position, and Financial Consolidation data products
Write and maintain pipeline code on OneLake/Lakehouse following medallion architecture patterns (Bronze/Silver/Gold) established by the Technical Lead
Participate in code reviews and apply BOD and engagement‑level coding standards consistently
Conduct unit and integration testing for pipeline outputs; document data flows and transformation logic
Coordinate with client‑side Fabric/Azure engineers to align ingestion patterns and shared schemas
Support documentation of data lineage, transformation logic, and pipeline metadata throughout the engagement


Requirements


5–6 years of data engineering experience with hands‑on pipeline development across multi‑source environments
Proficiency with Microsoft Fabric (OneLake, Lakehouse, Notebooks, Data Factory) or a comparable Azure data stack (ADF, ADLS Gen2, Databricks/Spark)
Solid SQL and PySpark skills with working knowledge of Delta Lake table formats
Experience building end‑to‑end pipelines for multi‑source integration
Familiarity with Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion architecture patterns
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field (or equivalent experience)


Preferred Qualifications


Exposure to ERP or CTRM source system data (SAP, Irely, or similar commodity/trading platforms)
Microsoft Azure data certification (DP‑203 or equivalent)
Experience in commodity trading, logistics, or financial services data environments
Basic


Salary: $ 5,500 - 7,200 [ USD ] monthly

Background checks may be required for certain positions/projects.

Blue Orange Digital is an equal-opportunity employer.

Blue Orange Digital is seeking a Mid‑Level Data Engineer to join a 26‑week enterprise data warehouse implementation for a global commodities trading and supply chain company, embedded full‑time from Week 3 through Week 22.

You will build and maintain Bronze and Silver layer pipelines on Microsoft Fabric, implementing ingestion from multi‑source ERP and CTRM systems and transformations that feed Gold‑layer data products across trading, risk, and finance domains.

Working within a project‑based delivery team alongside a Senior Data Engineer and Solutions Architect, you will contribute to a structured, phase‑gated delivery with clear milestones and real production output.

Responsibilities

  • Implement Bronze ingestion pipelines from source systems including CTRM instances (Irely) and ERP/SAP S/4HANA feeds using Microsoft Fabric Data Factory
  • Build Silver‑layer transformation logic in Fabric notebooks using PySpark and SQL on Delta Lake tables, covering cleansing, standardization, and business rule application
  • Support development of Gold‑layer outputs aligned to Trader P&L, Global Position, and Financial Consolidation data products
  • Write and maintain pipeline code on OneLake/Lakehouse following medallion architecture patterns (Bronze/Silver/Gold) established by the Technical Lead
  • Participate in code reviews and apply BOD and engagement‑level coding standards consistently
  • Conduct unit and integration testing for pipeline outputs; document data flows and transformation logic
  • Coordinate with client‑side Fabric/Azure engineers to align ingestion patterns and shared schemas
  • Support documentation of data lineage, transformation logic, and pipeline metadata throughout the engagement

Requirements

  • 5–6 years of data engineering experience with hands‑on pipeline development across multi‑source environments
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Fabric (OneLake, Lakehouse, Notebooks, Data Factory) or a comparable Azure data stack (ADF, ADLS Gen2, Databricks/Spark)
  • Solid SQL and PySpark skills with working knowledge of Delta Lake table formats
  • Experience building end‑to‑end pipelines for multi‑source integration
  • Familiarity with Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion architecture patterns
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field (or equivalent experience)

Preferred Qualifications

  • Exposure to ERP or CTRM source system data (SAP, Irely, or similar commodity/trading platforms)
  • Microsoft Azure data certification (DP‑203 or equivalent)
  • Experience in commodity trading, logistics, or financial services data environments
  • Basic

Salary: $ 5,500 - 7,200 [ USD ] monthly

Background checks may be required for certain positions/projects.

Blue Orange Digital is an equal-opportunity employer.

Title: Senior Data Analyst (Relocate to Cyprus)
Description:
We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our Player Care team at Scorewarrior. The Player Care team is at the forefront of providing customer service to our players, as well as safeguarding the community integrity through enforcing Fair & Safe Play, improving moderation efforts, and ensuring operational excellence while at it.




Scorewarrior is a game developer and publisher based in Limassol, Cyprus. The company focuses on the 4X strategy genre, with an ambitious goal to grow its hit game, Total Battle, to become the #1 strategy game in the world, and this role is key to achieving this. Our team consists of 200+ passionate, talented, self-driven, and continually developing professionals who love making high-quality games.




Our product: We are currently working on Total Battle — an MMO 4X strategy game with a deep political system and lots of player interaction. Players take on the role of a commander and need to develop their city, forge alliances, fight against monsters, and conquer other players. We're constantly developing new game mechanics, and the project is continuing to grow.




At the heart of our Player Care department, our mission is to excite our players with exceptional customer care, ensuring they feel valued and supported throughout their journey with us.




What you’ll do

Build and maintain the single "Source of Truth" semantic data layer for the entire Player Care department, enabling non-technical leads to perform ad-hoc analysis.
Design, build, and maintain reliable metrics dashboards tailored to the operational needs of different Support and Trust & Safety teams.
Ensure all operational metrics (CSAT, SLA, etc.) are clearly defined, reliable, and calculated correctly across all reports.
Assist and mentor the support team with SQL query composition and data literacy.
Conduct A/B tests to measure the effect of Support and Trust & Safety initiatives on relevant departmental and broader business metrics.
Analyze the correlation between support quality, bug-related friction, and subsequent player behavior, with a strong focus on VIP cohorts.
Partner with the safety team to analyze data for fraud detection, including social engineering and account sharing.
Perform time-series forecasting for workload volume based on the LiveOps calendar to optimize shift headcount and SLA coverage.
As a future ambition, build and maintain an early warning predictive model for VIP player churn.




What you have

Strong SQL skills and data modeling experience
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Tableau or equivalents)
Proven experience in product or operational analytics
Ability to design analytical events and metrics aligned with product goals
Strong analytical thinking and ability to translate data into actionable insights
Confident knowledge of mathematical statistics and probability theory
Hands-on experience with causal inference and A/B testing
Patience and willingness to advocate for data literacy and help operational teams learn basic data concepts




Nice to haves

Hands-on experience with ClickHouse and dbt
Experience analyzing LiveOps features, complex game economies, or VIP player behavior, ideally in mid-core or 4x strategy games.
Experience working with Customer Support or Trust & Safety (fraud detection, toxicity)
Experience working with large player bases and complex event-driven systems




Where you’ll work

We value face-to-face communication, and we strongly believe that genuine human interactions move us closer to our ambitious goal. This is part of our culture, and this is why this job offers no remote or hybrid work opportunities.

You will be working in Limassol, Cyprus. If you’re not from around here, fear not! Our dedicated relocation team will provide comprehensive support helping you and your family move to Limassol.




What we offer

The best team in the world!
An excellent remuneration package, including a 13th salary.
Fully-covered relocation to Limassol, Cyprus for you and your family.
A modern and sunny office space close to the sea.
Private health insurance for you and your family members.
A monthly school allowance for your children at pre-school and school-age.
Lunch compensation.
Personal development opportunities paid for by the company: professional and language courses, books, etc.
Bonuses for outstanding results, years worked, getting married, and the birth of a child.
Monthly office parties and epic, annual offsite company birthday and Christmas parties.

Title: Senior Data Analyst (all genders)
Description:
THE ROLE & THE TEAM

As a Senior Data Analyst in the Logistics Sustainability team, you will play a pivotal role in building the data infrastructure required for complex regulatory compliance, specifically Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation (ESPR). You will be responsible for connecting and curating highly fragmented datasets across systems, ensuring a reliable, auditable, and automated flow of information, and establishing a single source of truth that enables business-critical compliance and sustainability decisions.

You’ll work at the exact intersection of data engineering, compliance analytics, and logistics—translating complex operational data and material compositions into automated, audit-ready reporting structures. This is an opportunity to shape how one of Europe’s largest platforms manages its regulatory risk and measures its environmental impact through high-quality, scalable data solutions.

INCLUSIVE BY DESIGN

If you think you have what it takes, we encourage you to apply even if you don't meet every single requirement. You may just be the right candidate for this or other roles!

At Zalando, our vision is to be the leading pan-European ecosystem for fashion and lifestyle e-commerce – one that thrives on diversity and is truly inclusive by design. We believe that diverse teams fuel innovation and creativity, and we actively seek out talent from all backgrounds.

We actively seek to reduce bias in our hiring and employment processes, focusing on your qualifications, skills, and contributions. To support this, we kindly ask that you refrain from including personal details such as your photo, age, or marital status in your CV, ensuring a fair and equitable evaluation based solely on your abilities and potential.

We are committed to providing an exceptional and accessible candidate experience for everyone. If you require any accommodations to support you throughout the hiring process, please let us know – we are here to assist you.

Discover more about our commitment to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace https //jobs.zalando.com/en/our-culture/diversity-and-inclusion

WHAT WE’D LOVE YOU TO DO (AND LOVE DOING)



Lead the development of reliable, curated data products specifically designed to meet stringent EPR and environmental regulatory reporting frameworks across our logistics network.
Collaborate with cross-functional partners (Compliance, Legal, Sustainability, and Tech) to translate evolving regulatory rules into automated technical data models.
Design, monitor, and improve data pipelines that map product attributes (materials, weights, packaging components) to specific EPR fee structures and compliance logic.
Act as a data translator between legal compliance, business teams, and core engineering, ensuring data definitions match strict regulatory criteria.
Drive impact by transforming fragmented supply chain data into an auditable, high-integrity single source of truth for carbon reduction and compliance reporting.



WE’D LOVE TO MEET YOU IF



You have strong SQL and Python skills and deep experience working with large-scale data systems, data lakes, and dimensional modeling.
You bring experience working with regulatory data structures—ideally mapping data for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and packaging registers, or from working within highly regulated industries like finance, tax, or health tech.
You understand the unique challenges of regulatory data, such as maintaining historical audit trails, managing data gaps in product attributes, and handling multi-jurisdictional reporting rules.
You’ve worked with enterprise BI tools (MicroStrategy, Power BI, Tableau) and know how to build reliable, automated exception-reporting dashboards for data quality tracking.
You can independently manage technical challenges while engaging confidently with both technical teams and non-technical regulatory stakeholders.
You have a proven ability to leverage modern data techniques, such as exploring or utilizing LLMs for unstructured data categorization, data enrichment, or anomaly detection to automate data cleaning.



OUR OFFER

Zalando provides a range of benefits, here’s an overview of what you can expect. Ask your Talent Acquisition Partner to learn more about what we offer.



27 days of holiday a year to start for full-time employees
2 paid volunteering days a year
Hybrid working model with up to 60% remote per week, actual practice is up to each team to best support their collaboration
Work from abroad for up to 30 working days a year
Employee shares program
40% off fashion and beauty products sold and shipped by Zalando, 30% off Lounge by Zalando, discounts from external partners
Relocation assistance available (subject to prior agreement)
Family services, including counseling and support
Health and wellbeing options (including Wellhub, formerly Gympass)
Mental health support and coaching available
Drive your development through our training platform and biannual peer-to-peer review

Title: Data Analyst - Teilportfolio AIDA
Description:
Data Analyst - Teilportfolio AIDA (m/w/d)

DKB AG

Standort: Berlin

unbefristet

Teil- & Vollzeit möglich

ab sofort

Deine Aufgaben


Du arbeitest im Teilportfolio AIDA an der Schnittstelle von Business, Data & AI, Data Engineering und Plattformentwicklung und machst Daten für Analytics-, Reporting-, KI- und Automatisierungs-Use-Cases nutzbar
Du wirkst am Aufbau einer produktorientierten Datenplattform mit und hilfst, Daten als Data Products verständlich, qualitätsgesichert und wiederverwendbar bereitzustellen
Du übersetzt fachliche Anforderungen in Datenanforderungen, Data-Product-Spezifikationen, Transformationslogiken und Abnahmekriterien und bist Sparringspartnerin für Product Owner*innen, Data Product Owner*innen und Fachbereiche
Du analysierst DKB-interne und externe Datenquellen, definierst fachliche Regeln für Integration, Harmonisierung und Qualitätssicherung und arbeitest an Data Products wie Person, Konto, Einkommen oder weiteren bankfachlichen Datenprodukten
Du schlägst die Brücke zwischen fachlichem Datenverständnis und technischer Implementierung, begleitest die Umsetzung in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Data Engineers und testest neue Datenprodukte, Datenlogiken und Plattformartefakte fachlich auf Herz und Nieren


Dein Profil


Du hast einen Abschluss in (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Statistik, Mathematik, Wirtschaftswissenschaften oder einem vergleichbaren quantitativen Studiengang und bringst relevante Berufserfahrung als Data Analyst*in oder Analytics Engineer mit
Du verstehst fachliche Anforderungen schnell, kannst sie strukturieren, priorisieren und in belastbare Datenanforderungen, Analysekonzepte und (technische) Umsetzungsschritte übersetzen
Du bringst sehr gute SQL-Kenntnisse mit, kannst Datenmodelle, Datenflüsse und Transformationslogiken sicher analysieren, validieren und fachlich bewerten und so analytische Fragestellungen von der Datenexploration bis zur Ergebnisaufbereitung begleiten
Begriffe wie Data Product, Data Contract, Data Product Factory und Data Marketplace sind für dich idealerweise keine Fremdwörter - oder du hast Lust, diese Themen aktiv mitzugestalten
Du arbeitest sicher mit Git, Jira und Confluence und bewegst dich gern in einem agilen, interdisziplinären Umfeld


Die DKB steht für Vielfalt und Chancengerechtigkeit. Egal, woher du kommst, wie alt du bist, welches Geschlecht, welche Genderidentität, sexuelle Orientierung oder Religion du hast, ob mit oder ohne Behinderung - wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung!

Deine Benefits und mehr


Attraktive Vergütung (tariflich oder außertariflich)
Flexibles und mobiles Arbeiten – temporär auch im EU-Ausland
Du hast die Wahl: Benefits Pass, Jobticket oder Jobrad
Moderne Technik- ausstattung und Tool Stack
Individuelle Qualifi- zierungsmöglichkeiten
Duz-Kultur und kein Dresscode
Externe Mitarbeitenden- beratung
Angebote zum Gesundbleiben
Weitere Extras wie z.B. betriebliche Altersvorsorge und Versicherungsschutz


Immer für dich ansprechbar

Axel Zimmermann

Chapter Lead

axel.zimmermann@dkb.de

Deutsche Kreditbank AG

Du möchtest mehr zur DKB erfahren?

Dann schau auf unserer Karriereseite vorbei.

Title: Data Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
About us

At Venatic Intelligence, we build software for complex data analysis, focusing on the challenge of making sense of massive, fragmented, and unstructured datasets. To address this, we develop applications that integrate disparate data sources, resolve complex entities, and map hidden networks.




We are looking for a Data Engineer (m/w/d) to join our team in Berlin. You will play a central role in expanding our core architecture, connecting new data streams, and building the analytical tools that power our applications.




What you'll do

Design and build robust data pipelines that extract, clean, and load complex datasets from highly diverse sources.
Engineer custom data acquisition tools that rely on proxy-routed networks to securely navigate and extract information from complex digital environments.
Manage and scale our core database architecture. You will work with graph databases (e.g., Neo4j) for entity resolution and search indices (e.g., Elasticsearch) for rapid retrieval.
Develop resilient APIs and backend services to seamlessly integrate new capabilities into our platform.
Build and refine specialized analytical dashboards and UI components using Vue.js/Nuxt 3, allowing users to interact directly with complex data models.
Deploy, optimize, and secure our infrastructure across various hyperscalers (e.g., AWS, GCP, OCI).
Take technical ownership of your projects, from initial architecture decisions to deployment, using standard workflows (e.g., GitHub, Jira, Confluence).




What we're looking for

Roughly 2 years of hands-on experience in software engineering, data engineering, or backend development.
Strong programming skills in Node.js, TypeScript, and Python.
Demonstrable experience in building data pipelines, handling unstructured data, or developing specialized data extraction tools.
Solid understanding of data modeling. You should know your way around NoSQL, graph (e.g., Neo4j), and search (e.g., Elasticsearch) architectures.
Practical frontend experience, ideally with Vue.js/Nuxt 3, to build intuitive interfaces for complex data.
Comfortable managing cloud environments (e.g., GCP, OCI, AWS) and navigating modern engineering toolchains.
You are a pragmatic problem-solver. You care about building secure, highly performant software that works reliably in the real world.
Fluent English. Proficiency in German is a plus, but not required.




What we offer

A permanent role in a fast-growing, technology-driven firm.
The opportunity to build high-impact software and tackle complex architectural challenges.
Close mentorship within a small team of highly skilled engineers and analysts.
Competitive compensation.
28 days of annual leave.
Flexible working hours with the option to work remotely.
A tailored benefits package that fits your personal needs.




To apply, please send your CV and a brief introduction to hr@venatic-intelligence.com.




Venatic Intelligence is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, and we believe that a range of perspectives, experiences, and languages makes our work sharper and our analysis stronger. We hire on the basis of merit and are committed to equal opportunity for all candidates, regardless of background, identity, or circumstance.

  • Fluent English. Proficiency in German is a plus, but not required.


What we offer

  • A permanent role in a fast-growing, technology-driven firm.
  • The opportunity to build high-impact software and tackle complex architectural challenges.
  • Close mentorship within a small team of highly skilled engineers and analysts.
  • Competitive compensation.
  • 28 days of annual leave.
  • Flexible working hours with the option to work remotely.
  • A tailored benefits package that fits your personal needs.


To apply, please send your CV and a brief introduction to hr@venatic-intelligence.com.


Venatic Intelligence is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, and we believe that a range of perspectives, experiences, and languages makes our work sharper and our analysis stronger. We hire on the basis of merit and are committed to equal opportunity for all candidates, regardless of background, identity, or circumstance.

Title: Working Student CTO Office (m/f/d)
Description:
1KOMMA5°

At 1KOMMA5°, we pursue a clear vision: Living on wind and sunlight forever for free. To make this a reality, we are building the energy system of the future with Heartbeat AI. Want to be part of it?

We bring together regional craftsmanship and scalable software: We don't think of solar, batteries, heat pumps, and e-mobility as isolated components, but control them as an intelligent, integrated overall system in our virtual power plant. Directly connected to the electricity market – in real time, fully automated. This way, energy is used when it is available from renewables and particularly cost-effective. By 2030, our goal is to transition 1.5 million households to renewable energies. Over 3,000 people are working towards this every day, at more than 80 locations worldwide, from Finland to Australia.

Want to take responsibility and build solutions that truly matter? Apply now and help us shape the energy world of tomorrow.

Learn more about our Product & Tech team!

Your mission

The CTO Office at Heartbeat AI GmbH sits at the intersection of product, engineering, organizational design, and strategic operations. As Working Student, you will report to the Chief of Staff and get direct exposure to how a fast-scaling tech company operates from the inside


Own recurring org formats: You take end-to-end responsibility for recurring formats — from preparation and coordination through to execution
Support active initiatives: You assist the CTO Office on cross-functional projects with research, documentation, and follow-through on open items
Plan and coordinate onsites and team events: You handle logistics, scheduling, and follow-through for CTO Office and leadership onsites in collaboration with HR and ops


Your profile


You are currently enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's program; ideally in business, tech, or a related field
You are organized and reliable; you take ownership of tasks and follow through without being reminded
You communicate with clarity in English
You are proactive; you spot what needs to be done and act on it
You are comfortable working in a fast-moving environment with multiple parallel topics
You have a genuine interest in how tech organizations and product teams work
Previous working student or internship experience in a startup or tech company is a plus


Benefits


You are part of an international, dynamic, and highly motivated team of people who have proven to make things happen
With your work, you accelerate the "energy transition" and hence have a direct impact on our climate
Work with and learn from other super-smart colleagues
You will enjoy direct contact with core decision-makers
You will enjoy the best chances of entering full-time in one of Europe’s most thriving scaleups
You work remotely (Germany-wide), with offices in Hamburg, Berlin or Munich
Create a healthy balance alongside your work and enjoy all the benefits of the EGYM Wellpass
Benefits and discounts are yours with Futurebens
Whether city bike or e-bike - be flexible with our job bike leasing and do something good for the environment at the same time


About Us

"At 1KOMMA5°, our vision is to accelerate CO2 neutral life for all! We created an easy one-stop-shop to buy and install solar, charging stations and heat pumps, with the purpose of liberating all European homes from the shackles of fossil fuels. With Heartbeat, we make clean energy smarter. Connecting our customers into one powerful clean energy operating system to enable flexible tariffs and to ultimately follow the rhythm of wind and sun!" - Philipp Schröder (CEO 1KOMMA5°)

Title: Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d) - Berlin
Description:
Location: Come and join us in Hamburg, Berlin or Barcelona!

Freenow by Lyft empowers smarter mobility decisions helping people to move freely and cities to thrive.

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d) to collaborate with our operational squads. In this role you will be able to become an expert in our business and data analysis systems and environment to allow you to champion data-driven decisions as part of the Data Operations team.

Be ready to work in a multinational, diverse, highly motivated and collaborative team of passionate analysts who strive for excellence and like to have fun. Are you ready for your next ride?

YOUR DAILY ADVENTURES WILL INCLUDE:


Focus on analysing and presenting recommendations/process changes/new initiatives to management using insights derived from data.
Use your technical skills such as actively engaging in configuration and programming in the data environment, querying data via SQL from the data lake, automating data processes, deep dive analysis in Python and adjusting ETL tasks.
Bringing new insights and championing them using your analyses, dashboards, and good storytelling! We value ideas over hierarchy, and there's always a new path to follow if your idea is convincing and well-backed with data.
Develop monitoring tools to measure performance and help the business achieve its objectives.
Create queries to answer operational and business questions across our many operational teams to support decisions.
Communicate results in a clear and impactful manner.


TO BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE:


Educated to Degree Level or higher in an analytical discipline such as Engineering, Computer Science or Economics
5+ years of experience in an analytical role
You have curiosity in your essence, which motivates you to answer WHY things happened, not only scratching the surface, but aiming for well-founded actionable results
Curiosity about AI and its practical applications in analytics, experience using LLMs or AI-powered tools in a data context
Excellent technical skills including SQL knowledge, familiarity with visualisation tools (Metabase in particular is a plus) and python/R
Enthusiastic, outgoing and personable - you have the ability to think on your feet, work independently and also proactively with others to find solutions to issues
You understand how to engage and influence decision makers and have excellent communication skills
Driven - you are motivated to find solutions to problems and constantly go the extra mile
Strong communication and interpersonal skills as well as excellent written and spoken English


BENEFITS & PERKS IN A NUTSHELL:


Flexible working arrangements
LinkedIn Learning
Sabbatical & special leave policies
WeRoad partnership
Birthday, 24th + 31st December off
Short term EU work policy
Mobility Credit
Health Insurance
Employee assistance program


DIVERSITY, EQUITY & INCLUSION:

FREE NOW is an equal opportunity employer and we consider qualified applicants regardless of race, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability or age.

We want you to grow and evolve, bring your true self to work.

ABOUT US:

Freenow by Lyft empowers smarter mobility decisions, helping people to move freely and cities to thrive. Through our multi-mobility app, we feature broad options for everyone across 9 European markets and over 180 cities. Millions of passengers can access services including taxis, private hire vehicles, carsharing, car rental, e-scooters, e-bikes, e-mopeds, and public transport within a single app.

In July 2025 Freenow was acquired by Lyft, a global mobility platform, which connects riders and drivers for billions of rides across North America and Europe. Together, Freenow and Lyft are creating a more caring and connected world, with transportation for everyone. We are a global, diverse, highly motivated, and collaborative team that strives for excellence and likes to have fun. Ready for your next ride?

Title: Data Analyst (m/w/d) – Industrial & Automotive Consulting
Description:
Beschreibung

Du willst im Bereich Data Science & Analytics dort arbeiten, wo es wirklich zählt – an komplexen Industrieprojekten mit direktem Einfluss auf Entscheidungen auf Management-Ebene?

Bei DRIVE Consulting arbeitest du an anspruchsvollen Fragestellungen führender Industrieunternehmen. Du unterstützt dabei, datengetriebene Entscheidungen in Transformations- und Beratungsprojekten umzusetzen. Unsere Analysen und Dashboards fließen direkt in das Reporting und die Entscheidungsgrundlagen unserer Kunden ein.

In dieser Rolle arbeitest du hands-on an Analytics-Lösungen und unterstützt bei der Entwicklung von Dashboards, der Analyse komplexer Daten sowie beim Aufbau von Datenstrukturen und -pipelines. Dabei begleitest du Projekte von der Anforderungsanalyse über die Konzeption bis zur Umsetzung und entwickelst deine fachlichen und methodischen Fähigkeiten kontinuierlich weiter.

Deine Aufgaben


Du arbeitest an Analytics-Workstreams in Kundenprojekten und übernimmst Verantwortung für eigene Aufgaben und Teilprojekte
Gemeinsam mit deinem Team entwickelst und betreibst du Datenpipelines zwischen Cloud-Quellen und Analytics- bzw. Dashboard-Tools und stellst deren Qualität, Automatisierung und Performance sicher (insbesondere über SQL)
Du konzipierst und entwickelst Dashboards mit Fokus auf Usability und klare Entscheidungsunterstützung für das Management
Du analysierst komplexe und heterogene Datenstrukturen, bereitest Daten auf und führst verschiedene Datenquellen effizient zusammen
Du führst Ad-hoc-Analysen und explorative Auswertungen durch und bereitest Ergebnisse adressatengerecht auf
Du arbeitest eng im Projektteam und mit relevanten Stakeholdern zusammen und treibst die Umsetzung datengetriebener Lösungen aktiv voran
Du unterstützt bei Architekturfragen, Priorisierungen und der Weiterentwicklung von Analytics-Lösungen
Du bringst eigene Ideen ein und entwickelst dich fachlich und methodisch kontinuierlich weiter


Dein Profil


Du hast erste Berufserfahrung (ca. 1–3 Jahre) im Bereich Data Analytics, Data Science, Data Engineering oder einem vergleichbaren datengetriebenen Umfeld
Du verfügst über gute Kenntnisse in Python und bist in der Lage, komplexe Datenstrukturen eigenständig zu analysieren und aufzubereiten
Du hast erste Erfahrung im Aufbau von Datenpipelines und/oder im Dashboarding gesammelt
Du bringst Erfahrung im Umgang mit BI-Tools (z. B. AWS QuickSight, Grafana, Tableau, etc.) mit
Du arbeitest analytisch und strukturiert und hast Freude daran, komplexe Fragestellungen zu lösen
Du denkst dich schnell in neue Themen ein und entwickelst dich fachlich kontinuierlich weiter
Idealerweise hast du erste Berührungspunkte mit Prozessen in der Automobilindustrie oder im industriellen Umfeld
Du arbeitest gerne im Team, kommunizierst klar und trittst auch im Austausch mit Kunden sicher auf
Du bist bereit, regelmäßig vor Ort bei unseren Kunden zu arbeiten


Unser Angebot an dich

Bei DRIVE Consulting triffst du auf ein junges, wachsendes Team, dessen Herz für technische Beratungsprojekte in der Industrie schlägt – insbesondere im Automotive-Umfeld.

Dich erwartet ein Umfeld mit hohem fachlichen Anspruch, schneller Lernkurve und individueller Weiterentwicklung. Du arbeitest eng mit erfahrenen Kolleg:innen zusammen, übernimmst früh Verantwortung für eigene Themen und entwickelst deine fachlichen und methodischen Fähigkeiten kontinuierlich weiter.

Offenheit, Vertrauen und Teamspirit prägen unsere Zusammenarbeit – in einem motivierten Team mit klarer Umsetzungsorientierung.

Außerdem Erwarten Dich


Individuelles Coaching und persönliches Buddy-Programm für deinen Einstieg und deine kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung
Dienstwagen oder Jobrad bzw. Jobticket – flexibel nach deinen Bedürfnissen
Täglicher Lunch-Zuschuss (6 €)
EGYM Wellpass für Fitness- und Gesundheitsangebote
Attraktive Mitarbeiterrabatte über Corporate Benefits
Zuschuss zur betrieblichen Altersvorsorge
Obst, Kaffee und Erfrischungsgetränke im Office
Regelmäßige Teamevents zur Stärkung des Teamzusammenhalts
Monatliches Happy Friday Frühstück
100 € Spendenbudget zur freien Verfügung
Sabbatical-Optionen


Über uns

DRIVE Consulting GmbH ist eine Unternehmensberatung mit Hauptsitz in Aachen sowie Standorten in München, Berlin, Peking und Shanghai. Unser Herz schlägt für technische Beratungsprojekte mit Fokus auf die Automobil- und Industriebranche. Zu unseren Kunden zählen sowohl deutsche Premium OEMs als auch junge, aufstrebende Automobilhersteller sowie große und mittelständische Industrieunternehmen.

Unser Name steht für unseren klaren Fokus auf die Mobilitätsbranche und repräsentiert gleichzeitig unseren Spirit – denn wir sind ein Team, in dem jede*r Einzelne DRIVE hat.

Wir stehen unseren Kunden als Wegbereiter ins Unbekannte und als Katalysator für Innovationen zur Seite. Denn wir sind überzeugt, dass man nur mit dem Mut, neue Wege einzuschlagen, einzigartige Erfolgsgeschichten schreiben kann.

Wir schlagen Brücken zwischen kleinen Ideenschmieden und großen Konzernen sowie zwischen Management und Technologieexpert*innen.

Durch den engen und partnerschaftlichen Austausch mit unseren Kunden entwickeln wir maßgeschneiderte und individuelle Lösungen. Ein entscheidender Erfolgsfaktor ist dabei unser einzigartiges Partnernetzwerk aus Industrie und Forschung. Dank unserer Nähe zu namhaften Universitäten wie der RWTH Aachen verbinden wir Wissenschaft und Praxis.

Begeisterung wird bei uns großgeschrieben und bestimmt unseren Arbeitsalltag – denn sie ist der beste Antrieb.

Title: Junior Consultant (F/M/X)
Description:
Tekkr is seeking a high-potential Junior Consultant to operate at the intersection of consulting rigor and startup execution, supporting fast-scaling tech companies on Product, Technology, and organizational challenges.




In this role, you will support senior consultants on client engagements with tech leaders (CTOs, CPOs, founders) by producing structured analyses, executive-ready content, and workshop materials, while progressively taking ownership of defined workstreams within the first months. You will also contribute to internal initiatives such as playbook development and the Tekkr app, in a fast-paced and pragmatic environment.




Overall, the position offers accelerated learning, strong analytical training, and early ownership, right at the intersection of professional consulting and the fast-paced startup world.




---




Our mission: What if… your work empowered tech teams across the world?

Technology helps address the world’s biggest challenges from climate crisis to inefficiency in governments. However, many companies and teams are bogged down by their own inefficiencies and struggle to develop technology effectively. Tekkr’s mission is to empower tech teams and their leaders to achieve their full potential! Join tekkr and become a positive multiplier for techies across the globe!




We work primarily with other, cutting-edge startups (Parloa, Orbem, Forto, Vimcar...) and help them get even further ahead. So not only will you work with a kick-ass Tekkr team, but also get real-life exposure to amazing customers and collaborate with their tech leadership on some of their most exciting challenges.


Tasks


Client delivery and consulting support (~80%)





Problem-solving: Apply strong problem-structuring to break down complex organizational and tech challenges (e.g., frameworks, hypotheses, action plans)


Content preparation: Build rigorous quantitative and qualitative analyses (e.g., slide decks, spreadsheets, reports, process blueprints, impact dashboards) transforming interview transcripts, market research, or data inputs into structured deliverables


Meetings facilitation: Support senior consultants in preparing and facilitating client-facing meetings, e.g., diagnostic workshops, discovery interviews, cross-teams workshops





The Junior Consultant will first be expected to support the senior consulting staff (for ~3 months) and will be asked to progressively take ownership of clearly defined workstreams (e.g., challenge diagnostics, prioritization frameworks, initiative tracking). From Day 1, they will be asked to ensure clarity, logic, and executive-level quality in all outputs.




Internal initiatives and product contribution (~20%)





Support internal product efforts related to the Tekkr app (e.g., collection of user insights, structuring of client case studies)


Help consolidate and improve internal consulting processes and templates, ensuring cross-pollination of knowledge assets


Participate in internal strategy, growth, and operational initiatives (e.g., go-to-market and content creation, proposals, training materials)





Autonomy and execution mindset





Manage daily priorities in a fast-paced environment with multiple parallel topics


Work independently on tasks while maintaining high quality and speed


Operate pragmatically with a strong bias for execution over over-engineering



Requirements


Required





Degree in Business, Technology, Engineering, or a related field (minimum Bachelor’s Degree, Master’s Degree preferred)


Initial experience (internships or full-time) in consulting, startups, venture capital funds, or strategy-related roles


Strong analytical capabilities (e.g., Excel/Google Sheets, structured analysis, synthesis of complex information)


Excellent written communication skills (clear, structured, executive-ready), in English (required) and German or any other European language


Strong ability to leverage AI tooling (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Tekkr app)


Ability to work effectively in a team-oriented environment and manage multiple tasks simultaneously


Travel to client sites as required (estimated <30%)





Preferred





Internship or experience in a consulting firm (strategy, boutique, or tech consulting)


Exposure to startups, SaaS, or tech organizations (e.g., Product, Ops, BizOps, Founder’s Associate roles)


Strong interest in Product, Technology, and organizational scaling topics


Familiarity with analytical tools (Google Sheets, Excel, Airtable, Jira, etc.)


Experience preparing presentations for senior stakeholders or executives


Interest in B2B SaaS, tech productivity, and scaling tech organizations



Benefits


Tekkr offers a unique career path for high-potential graduates who want the rigor of consulting without losing the speed and ownership of a startup.




You will:





Work directly with tech leaders (CTOs, CPOs, founders)


Develop top-tier structuring, analytical, and communication skills, leveraging our team of senior consultants with MBB background (McKinsey, BCG)


Gain early ownership faster than in traditional consulting firms


Operate in a hybrid environment combining consulting excellence with startup pragmatism





The hiring process

We respect your time, but we also validate rigorously for the right fit.





Standardized test (1h)


Screening (30m): Short call with Tekkr Lead Consultant to discuss your CV, assess motivation, communication, and baseline structuring ability.


Case interview + discussion (60m)


Take-home project [a couple of hours]: We give you a real-life scenario based on the Tekkr context to evaluate your discovery skills, strategic thinking and problem-solving.


Deliverable presentation [1 HR]: You present the outcome of your take-home assignment to the Tekkr team. Potentially on-site.


Founder call: Deep dive with Philipp Römer about the Junior Consultant role, your motivation, and the vision.


Decision





Ready to go? Apply now.

HTML:
Tekkr is seeking a high-potential Junior Consultant to operate at the intersection of consulting rigor and startup execution, supporting fast-scaling tech companies on Product, Technology, and organizational challenges.


In this role, you will support senior consultants on client engagements with tech leaders (CTOs, CPOs, founders) by producing structured analyses, executive-ready content, and workshop materials, while progressively taking ownership of defined workstreams within the first months. You will also contribute to internal initiatives such as playbook development and the Tekkr app, in a fast-paced and pragmatic environment.


Overall, the position offers accelerated learning, strong analytical training, and early ownership, right at the intersection of professional consulting and the fast-paced startup world.


---


Our mission: What if… your work empowered tech teams across the world?

Technology helps address the world’s biggest challenges from climate crisis to inefficiency in governments. However, many companies and teams are bogged down by their own inefficiencies and struggle to develop technology effectively. Tekkr’s mission is to empower tech teams and their leaders to achieve their full potential! Join tekkr and become a positive multiplier for techies across the globe!


We work primarily with other, cutting-edge startups (Parloa, Orbem, Forto, Vimcar...) and help them get even further ahead. So not only will you work with a kick-ass Tekkr team, but also get real-life exposure to amazing customers and collaborate with their tech leadership on some of their most exciting challenges.


Tasks

Client delivery and consulting support (~80%)



  • Problem-solving: Apply strong problem-structuring to break down complex organizational and tech challenges (e.g., frameworks, hypotheses, action plans)

  • Content preparation: Build rigorous quantitative and qualitative analyses (e.g., slide decks, spreadsheets, reports, process blueprints, impact dashboards) transforming interview transcripts, market research, or data inputs into structured deliverables

  • Meetings facilitation: Support senior consultants in preparing and facilitating client-facing meetings, e.g., diagnostic workshops, discovery interviews, cross-teams workshops


The Junior Consultant will first be expected to support the senior consulting staff (for ~3 months) and will be asked to progressively take ownership of clearly defined workstreams (e.g., challenge diagnostics, prioritization frameworks, initiative tracking). From Day 1, they will be asked to ensure clarity, logic, and executive-level quality in all outputs.


Internal initiatives and product contribution (~20%)



  • Support internal product efforts related to the Tekkr app (e.g., collection of user insights, structuring of client case studies)

  • Help consolidate and improve internal consulting processes and templates, ensuring cross-pollination of knowledge assets

  • Participate in internal strategy, growth, and operational initiatives (e.g., go-to-market and content creation, proposals, training materials)


Autonomy and execution mindset



  • Manage daily priorities in a fast-paced environment with multiple parallel topics

  • Work independently on tasks while maintaining high quality and speed

  • Operate pragmatically with a strong bias for execution over over-engineering


Requirements

Required



  • Degree in Business, Technology, Engineering, or a related field (minimum Bachelor’s Degree, Master’s Degree preferred)

  • Initial experience (internships or full-time) in consulting, startups, venture capital funds, or strategy-related roles

  • Strong analytical capabilities (e.g., Excel/Google Sheets, structured analysis, synthesis of complex information)

  • Excellent written communication skills (clear, structured, executive-ready), in English (required) and German or any other European language

  • Strong ability to leverage AI tooling (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Tekkr app)

  • Ability to work effectively in a team-oriented environment and manage multiple tasks simultaneously

  • Travel to client sites as required (estimated <30%)


Preferred



  • Internship or experience in a consulting firm (strategy, boutique, or tech consulting)

  • Exposure to startups, SaaS, or tech organizations (e.g., Product, Ops, BizOps, Founder’s Associate roles)

  • Strong interest in Product, Technology, and organizational scaling topics

  • Familiarity with analytical tools (Google Sheets, Excel, Airtable, Jira, etc.)

  • Experience preparing presentations for senior stakeholders or executives

  • Interest in B2B SaaS, tech productivity, and scaling tech organizations


Benefits

Tekkr offers a unique career path for high-potential graduates who want the rigor of consulting without losing the speed and ownership of a startup.


You will:



  • Work directly with tech leaders (CTOs, CPOs, founders)

  • Develop top-tier structuring, analytical, and communication skills, leveraging our team of senior consultants with MBB background (McKinsey, BCG)

  • Gain early ownership faster than in traditional consulting firms

  • Operate in a hybrid environment combining consulting excellence with startup pragmatism


The hiring process

We respect your time, but we also validate rigorously for the right fit.



  1. Standardized test (1h)

  2. Screening (30m): Short call with Tekkr Lead Consultant to discuss your CV, assess motivation, communication, and baseline structuring ability.

  3. Case interview + discussion (60m)

  4. Take-home project [a couple of hours]: We give you a real-life scenario based on the Tekkr context to evaluate your discovery skills, strategic thinking and problem-solving.

  5. Deliverable presentation [1 HR]: You present the outcome of your take-home assignment to the Tekkr team. Potentially on-site.

  6. Founder call: Deep dive with Philipp Römer about the Junior Consultant role, your motivation, and the vision.

  7. Decision


Ready to go? Apply now.

Title: Associate Partner - AI & Data - Life Sciences
Description:
Visium combines strategy, frontier AI, and deep regulated-industry expertise to build and deploy AI-native systems that reinvent how businesses operate - unlocking the value of AI at scale.

We're not a strategy firm that ships slide decks and walks away. Not a tech consultancy that builds what won't get adopted. Not a product company chasing problems it doesn't understand. We're all three at once: strategy, deep technical and integration expertise, and product, working as one. That combination is our flywheel. Consulting keeps us close to the hardest real-world problems. Products let us solve them at scale. Every engagement sharpens our products; every deployment makes the next engagement faster, deeper, and more certain.

We're obsessed with outcomes. While 95% of enterprise AI pilots never reach production, Visium is built to be the 5% delivering productised AI that compounds in value, not point solutions that deliver marginal gains. We are AI-native, not AI-adjacent. We don't bolt AI onto broken workflows; we redesign them with AI at the core.

Role

At Visium, you'll have the opportunity to take on an entrepreneurial role, contributing to the organization's growth and finding innovative ways to create meaningful impact for clients. Your mission will involve advising C-Level executives on their AI & Data strategies and guiding large-scale digital transformations. You'll act as a trusted advisor and a key source of strategic insight, overseeing and nurturing long-term client relationships while ensuring the highest quality of delivery.

As an Associate Partner you will be responsible for:


Creating new business opportunities and expanding existing ones. Rely on your extensive network and collaborate with our business development team to create new business opportunities in LifeScience
Engage with our client's C-level stakeholders to understand their Data and AI related challenges, developing solutions and support them in implementing and scaling them in a sustainable way
Lead and collaborate effectively within large cross-functional teams to review and analyze client requirements or challenges and provide leadership and direction to elevate our delivery capabilities.
Lead engagement teams in developing logical and insightful analyses; innovative & effective recommendations and high-quality deliverables


Depending on your previous experiences and appetite to grow, you will play an active role in practice development.

Requirements

What we are looking for


Director, Associate Partner/Partner level (or equivalent title) experience at a consulting firm
Data Science background and specialization across LifeScience & Healthcare are required.
Proven track record of developing new client relationships and generating business development opportunities that leads to successful client engagements
Demonstrated ability with the management of a portfolio of healthcare clients generating 7 figures in revenues in a consulting-like industry
Experience leading cross-functional teams and in-depth knowledge/defined involvement in helping frame, design and implement and scale solutions for challenging business situations
Exceptional intellectual and analytical curiosity, a creative visionary who can drive innovation
Ability to communicate complex ideas effectively - both verbally and in writing - in English. French and/or German are considered a plus.


Benefits

Benefits

What we offer


A competitive compensation package
A yearly education budget to steep your learning curve
A yearly sport budget because a fit body leads to a fit mind
A flexible working culture because your work-life balance matters to us
A position that enables you to have an impact on 1'000s of people, and the whole company's growth
An international, knowledgeable, and passionate team with a strong collaborative mindset


Check our LinkedIn and website to learn more about us & don't hesitate to contact us if you have any questions.

Title: Founders Associate
Description:
Als Founders Associate (m/w/d) arbeitest du eng mit unserem Gründerteam zusammen und bekommst einen einzigartigen Einblick in den Aufbau und die Weiterentwicklung eines Tech-Startups. Du übernimmst Verantwortung in strategischen und operativen Projekten und unterstützt bei vielfältigen Themen im Tagesgeschäft.


Strategische Zusammenarbeit: Enge Unterstützung der Gründer und des Leadership-Teams bei der Identifikation und Umsetzung von kommerziellen Initiativen, die das Wachstum des Unternehmens maßgeblich vorantreiben.
Projektverantwortung: Eigenverantwortliche Steuerung und Begleitung von strategisch relevanten Projekten mit Fokus auf Business Development, Sales Enablement und Prozessoptimierung.
Cross-funktionale Koordination: Du wirkst als zentrale Schnittstelle zwischen verschiedenen Teams (z.B. Sales, Product, Customer Success) und sorgst für eine zielgerichtete Abstimmung aller Aktivitäten.
Markt- und Wettbewerbsanalyse: Du beobachtest und analysierst relevante Markttrends sowie Kundenanforderungen und unterstützt so die datenbasierte Entscheidungsfindung.
Unterstützung von Wachstumsinitiativen: Du hilfst bei der Entwicklung und Optimierung von Go-to-Market Strategien und trägst zur Expansion in neue Märkte bei.
Vorbereitung von Präsentationen und Reportings: Du erstellst aussagekräftige Analysen, Entscheidungsvorlagen und Präsentationen für interne und externe Stakeholder.


Your profile


Nachgewiesene Erfahrung in einer ähnlichen Funktion, vorzugsweise in einem schnell wachsenden SaaS-Unternehmen oder gerne auch in der Managementberatung - plus wenn deine Aufgaben dort im kommerziellen Bereich lagen.
Du zeichnest dich durch strategisches Denken und die Fähigkeit aus, Projekte von der Konzeption bis zur Umsetzung zu begleiten.
Du bist kommunikativ stark und kannst funktionsübergreifende Teams motivieren und führen.
Du hast den Ehrgeiz, dich in einer dynamischen und schnell wachsenden Organisation weiterzuentwickeln, und möchtest langfristig Verantwortung übernehmen.
Verhandlungssichere Englischkenntnisse sind für dich selbstverständlich.
Du sprichst Deutsch auf muttersprachlichem Niveau.


Why us


Direkte Zusammenarbeit mit erfahrenen Gründern
Tiefer Einblick in den Aufbau und die Skalierung eines SaaS-Start-ups
Viel Verantwortung ab Tag 1
Flexible Arbeitszeiten & hybride Arbeitsweise
Offene, ehrliche und motivierte Teamkultur mit kurzen Entscheidungswegen
Aussicht auf Übernahme als Werkstudent:in oder in Festanstellung


Über uns

Cosuno – Wir revolutionieren die Baubranche durch Technologie.

Wir sind Cosuno – ein schnell wachsendes Tech-Startup, das die Baubranche mit unserer digitalen Plattform für Ausschreibungen und Beschaffungsprozesse effizienter und transparenter macht. Unser Ziel: die komplexesten Herausforderungen der Branche mit Innovation und Einfachheit zu lösen.

Warum Cosuno?

Künstliche Intelligenz ist der Schlüssel zu unserer Lösung: Sie analysiert Millionen von Preisdatenpunkten und hilft Bauunternehmen dabei, präzise und effiziente Angebote zu erstellen. Das spart unseren Kunden nicht nur Zeit, sondern hilft ihnen auch, unnötige Kosten zu vermeiden.

Aber für uns geht es nicht nur um Technologie – es geht um die Menschen, die sie vorantreiben. Bei Cosuno arbeitest du mit kreativen Köpfen zusammen, die die Baubranche neu gestalten. Wir glauben an Diversität, denn wir wissen, dass die besten Ideen aus unterschiedlichen Perspektiven entstehen. Ein integratives und exklusives Arbeitsumfeld ist für uns eine Selbstverständlichkeit.

Werde Teil unseres Teams – Gestalte die Zukunft der Baubranche.
Show more
