Title: Business Systems Data Scientist
Description:
NFI Parts™ is North America's most comprehensive organization providing parts, technical publications, training, and support for buses and motor coaches. NFI Parts is the parts division of NFI Group, providing replacement parts for New Flyer® transit buses, MCI® motor coaches, ADL buses, ARBOC® vehicles, as well as the product lines for the acquired NABI and Orion parts businesses. NFI Parts maintains extensive cross-references for parts and has established relationships with over 1,000 suppliers. Learn more at www.nfi.parts.

POSITION GOAL:

The position leads data‑driven initiatives by designing, testing, documenting, and operationalizing AI‑enabled and analytics solutions across Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake, and related enterprise systems. Responsibilities include coordinating business‑value projects, developing scalable data processes, supporting user adoption, and providing Snowflake administration—monitoring usage, optimizing performance, managing roles, and ensuring governance across platforms.

WHAT YOU WILL DO:


Work with various ERP and IOT data sources to build interactive and dynamic Power BI dashboards for data storytelling and stakeholder decision support
Perform data exploration, statistical analysis, and feature engineering to support AI initiatives
Contribute to the development of AI-driven tools and frameworks within the organization
Participate in cross-functional projects involving manufacturing, quality, supply chain and engineering
Update training, and documentation
Deliver user training to operating groups as required
Assist in managing Snowflake processes and costs


Requirements


Post secondary education in a business or STEM field or equivalent combination of education and experience
Any combination of training and/or certifications in Data Science, Statistics, and Artificial Intelligence
2+ years of experience in a data science, AI, or advanced analytics role
Strong proficiency in SQL, Python, Fabric/Power BI and Snowflake
Solid understanding of AI model evaluation and workflows
Deep knowledge of data warehousing concepts and cloud-based data platforms
Excellent communication skills to present data-driven insights clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Ability to work with minimum supervision and possesses initiative to seek improvements.
Exceptional data problem-solving and project management skills
Excellent communication skills, extremely organized, self-starter, detail oriented, strong computing and facilitation skills
Travel might be required for this position


Benefits

WHY JOIN OUR TEAM:


Competitive Wages
A continuous learning environment
Ability to advance your career with a growing company
NFI Group has been named a Manitoba Top Employer for 2020 and this is the tenth since the competition was established in 2007
Employee social committee organizes numerous events throughout the year, including summer barbecues, holiday festivities, sporting events and tournaments, and much more
Ongoing employee development through a variety of in-house training initiatives along with tuition subsidies for courses at outside institutions


OUR WHY

We exist to move people. Our mission is to design and deliver exceptional transportation solutions that are safe, accessible, efficient and reliable.

NFI Group is a leading independent global bus manufacturer providing a comprehensive suite of mass transportation solutions. News and information is available at www.nfigroup.com, www.newflyer.com, www.mcicoach.com, www.nfi.parts, www.alexander-dennis.com, www.arbocsv.com, and www.carfaircomposites.com.

Title: Data Integrity Solutions Analyst - Business Data Office – Data Integrity & Operations
Description:
Experience something Brilliantly Different with a career at MUFG Investor Services.  



MUFG Investor Services is a trusted partner to many of the world’s largest public and private funds, providing asset servicing and operational solutions built for alternatives. With over $1 trillion in client assets under administration, we offer fund administration, banking, payments, fund financing, foreign exchange overlay, corporate and regulatory services, custody, business consulting, and more. Operating from 17 locations worldwide, we help clients mitigate risk, enhance efficiency, and navigate the operational complexities of today’s investment management landscape. As a division of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s largest financial institutions with approximately $3 trillion in assets, we combine deep expertise with the strength and stability of a leading financial institution. To learn more, visit us at www.mufg-investorservices.com.





Job Description



You will support the design and enhancement of data integrity solutions across lineage, data quality, reconciliations, metadata, and Master Data Management (MDM). Working closely with technology, PMO, and business teams, you will analyse requirements, shape logical and functional solution designs, assess data impacts of change, and ensure new and enhanced capabilities align with enterprise data standards and enable trusted, well‑governed data across the organisation.



Translate business and data integrity needs into clear functional requirements for lineage, data quality, reconciliations, metadata, and MDM capabilities.
Contribute to the logical design of data integrity solutions, including data rules, lineage models, reconciliation logic, and metadata standards.
Perform data impact assessments on change initiatives, ensuring designs meet “data essentials” standards and best‑practice principles.
Assess proposed changes for impacts on data flows, ownership, controls, lineage, and quality, ensuring alignment with enterprise data architecture.
Partner with technology teams to refine solution designs, prepare functional specifications, support handover for build, and validate delivered functionality.
Use SQL and analytical techniques to explore datasets, validate assumptions, and provide evidence‑based inputs into solution design.
Support conceptual and logical data modelling activities to ensure alignment with enterprise data architecture.
Maintain clear documentation, including requirements, specifications, data flows, and test evidence.


Qualifications


5+ years’ experience in data analysis, data management, data architecture, or business analysis roles within complex enterprise environments.
Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills with advanced SQL and hands‑on experience working with complex datasets.
Experience analysing end‑to‑end data flows, including lineage, source‑to‑target mapping, integration patterns, and transformation logic.
Clear communication and stakeholder engagement skills, able to translate technical concepts and solution designs into concise, actionable requirements.
Solid understanding of data governance, metadata, ownership, and control concepts, and how they enable trusted and well‑managed data.
Experience contributing to the design or specification of data solutions such as lineage models, data quality rules, reconciliation logic, or MDM capabilities.
Exposure to data quality, lineage, metadata, or MDM tooling — including enterprise platforms (e.g., Collibra, Purview, Informatica) and open‑source tools (e.g., OpenLineage, Great Expectations).
Exposure to data integration technologies or APIs and an understanding of how data moves and transforms across systems.
Experience working in agile or iterative delivery environments, contributing to backlogs, refinement, and validation activities.
Experience using scripting or analytical languages (e.g., Python) to support analysis, validation, or light automation is desirable.


Additional Information



What’s in it for you to join MUFG Investor Services? 



Take a look at our careers site and you’ll find everything you’d expect from a career with the fastest-growing business at one of the world’s largest financial groups. Now take another look. Because it’s how we defy expectations that really defines us. You’ll feel that difference in all kinds of ways. Our vibrant CULTURE. Connected team. Love of innovation, laser client focus, and next-level LEARNING & DEVELOPMENT. Oh, and we really walk the talk when it comes to HYBRID WORKING.   



So, why settle for the ordinary?  Apply now for a Brilliantly Different career.

Title: Coordinator, Planning and Data Analysis
Description:
Union/Affiliation: Administrative and Professional Staff (APSA)

Pay range: $74,288 to $88,609 annually

SFU Department Description: Faculty of Education - Finance Office

Position Grade: 8

# of openings: 1

Biweekly Hours: 72




Who We Are




Simon Fraser University is a leading research university, advancing an inclusive and sustainable future. Our purpose – the essence of SFU – is to create and connect knowledge, learning and community for deeper understanding and meaningful impact. We are committed to fostering excellence, innovation, belonging and community in all that we do.




About The Role




The Coordinator, Planning and Data Analysis is responsible for providing leadership to strengthen Faculty-wide strategic enrolment management plans, objectives, and goals, including planning for optimum enrolments, and supplemental admissions activities and initiatives. The position supports the Faculty of Education by undertaking research; analyzing data; preparing reports; forecasting required and available resources; and forecasting enrolment and financial implications of curriculum changes. The role is responsible for providing quantitative and qualitative information extraction and analysis with regards to enrolment and financial implications to support decision making by the faculty's executive team and other stakeholders, and providing business solutions to support Faculty unit decisions to meet their Strategic Enrolment Management objectives.




Qualifications




Bachelor's degree in a quantitative science, business administration, social sciences, or a related discipline and five years related experience, including experience in research methods, statistical analysis, strategic enrolment management, financial management or an equivalent combination of education, training and experience.




Data & Systems Expertise: Good knowledge of data structures, system relationships, and enterprise­level information systems such as SIMS, HAP, and FINS/FAST.
Strategic Enrolment & Financial Acumen: Excellent understanding of strategic enrollment management practices, performance measurement, budget planning, and financial management.
Analytical Proficiency: Strong capabilities in data analysis, statistical research methods, and the use of Windows­based software, particularly Microsoft Office tools like pivot tables and databases.
Project Management: Excellent strategic planning, time management, and project management skills, including the ability to read and interpret complex project plans and specifications.
Information Synthesis: A proven ability to synthesize complex qualitative and quantitative information to formulate sound recommendations for senior leadership.




What We Offer




At SFU, our goal is to ensure our people are valued and supported by promoting a healthy work-life balance, professional growth and development, as well as a safe and respectful workplace. We offer continuing employees who belong in the Administrative & Professional Staff Association (APSA):




4 weeks’ vacation (prorated for the first year)*
Hybrid-work program for eligible positions
Employer paid defined benefit pension plan
On-campus tuition waiver for employees and their immediate family members*
Off-campus tuition reimbursements and professional development funds*
And more!
Prorated for part-time employees




Additional Information




This position is located at both SFU Burnaby and Surrey campuses.




Reports to the Associate Director, Finance.




Please include your cover letter and resume in one attachment.




SFU is an equity employer and strongly encourages applications from all qualified individuals including women, Indigenous Peoples, visible minorities, people of all sexual orientations and gender identities, persons with disabilities, and others who may contribute to the further diversification of the university.




We are committed to ensuring that the application and interview process is accessible to all applicants. If you require any assistance or accommodations, please contact pei_reception@sfu.ca.

At SFU, our goal is to ensure our people are valued and supported by promoting a healthy work-life balance, professional growth and development, as well as a safe and respectful workplace. We offer continuing employees who belong in the Administrative & Professional Staff Association (APSA):


  • 4 weeks’ vacation (prorated for the first year)*
  • Hybrid-work program for eligible positions
  • Employer paid defined benefit pension plan
  • On-campus tuition waiver for employees and their immediate family members*
  • Off-campus tuition reimbursements and professional development funds*
  • And more!
  • Prorated for part-time employees


Additional Information


This position is located at both SFU Burnaby and Surrey campuses.


Reports to the Associate Director, Finance.


Please include your cover letter and resume in one attachment.


SFU is an equity employer and strongly encourages applications from all qualified individuals including women, Indigenous Peoples, visible minorities, people of all sexual orientations and gender identities, persons with disabilities, and others who may contribute to the further diversification of the university.


We are committed to ensuring that the application and interview process is accessible to all applicants. If you require any assistance or accommodations, please contact pei_reception@sfu.ca.

Title: Supply Chain Data Analyst
Description:
Convergint is looking for a team-oriented, results-driven, Supply Chain Data Analyst, to join our amazing culture. As the Supply Chain Data Analyst, you will be responsible for maintaining vendor item master catalogs and provide reporting and analytics to support the source to pay process. This role involves tasks like data entry, updates, and resolving discrepancies, with a strong focus on data integrity and efficiency in operations. This role has an emphasis on support of the supply chain and sales teams, and motivation in building effective business relationships with vendors and customers alike.

Who You Are

You have a passion for providing world-class service to customers, colleagues and communities. You are a person of integrity with a commitment to growth, accountability and delivering results. You want to join an organization with a positive culture that embraces inclusion and allows everyone to be the best version of themselves at work and home.

Who We Are

With 20-years of proven growth and exceptional performance, our mission is to be our customer’s best service provider. We realize the importance of diversity in achieving that goal. Our company was built upon a solid foundation of 10 Values and Beliefs which drive our unmatched culture, making us the #1 global, serviced-based systems integrator in the industry. We take great pride in protecting the lives and assets of our customers and their communities with the solutions we provide.

What you’ll do with “Our Training and Your Experience”


Creating, updating, and managing item records within relevant systems.
Ensure the accuracy, completeness, and consistency of item data, including descriptions, classifications, units of measure, vendor information, pricing, and other relevant details.
Conduct regular audits to identify and correct errors, duplicates, or outdated records.
Manage the lifecycle of item records, including creation, updates, and decommissioning.
Support for item master data management, including troubleshooting issues and assisting with system enhancements.
Interact with suppliers, manufacturers, and distributors on an on-going basis, which may include negotiating prices, discounts, credit terms, and logistics.
Participating in the development and implementation of best practices for item master data management.
Work with Supply Chain leadership to develop and implement best practices, demand planning, and other optimizations in order to help grow the business and to ensure process transparency, fairness, impartiality, and confidentiality.
Collaborate with global counterparts to align with process standardization and consistency in reporting across all regions.
Provide reporting and data analytics to Supply Chain leadership to show trends, address discrepancies, and generate accurate forecasting.
Develop constructive and cooperative working relationships with internal stakeholders and vendors alike and maintain them over time.
Executes all work in accordance with Provincial/State, local regulations, general best practices as well as company health and safety policies and procedures.
Acts as “our customer’s best service provider” at all times thereby ensuring Convergint Technologies is the customer’s first choice for service.
Communicates with and works with the other team members consistently to improve overall operations of the Convergint Technology Center, and company.
Keeps up to date and informed on all company policies.
Presents a professional image of Convergint Technologies at all times in appearance and behavior.
Performs other duties and responsibilities as requested or required.


What You’ll Need


Strong attention to details.
Strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills and ability to deal with a wide cross section of people both internal and external to the business.
Excellent Excel, Word, and Outlook skills.
Excellent proficiency in financial software specifically related to purchase order entry, accounts payable, and inventory management.
Strong negotiation skills.
Strong financial knowledge and skills—ability to understand basic financials such as accounts receivable/payable, purchase orders, work orders, etc.
Excellent organizational skills and the ability to handle multiple projects tasks simultaneously.
Excellent attention to detail.
Strong flexibility to adapt to changing priorities, work processes, department structure, environment and technology in a dynamic work environment.
Self-driven and motivated to meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Strong analytical skills are necessary to resolve problems and look for solutions.
Strong ability to demonstrate independence, resourcefulness and tenacity.
Shows initiative – regularly engages in proactive behavior and looks for opportunities.


Requirements


Diploma and/or certificate in Business Administration or Purchasing required.
SCMP designation highly desirable.
Minimum of five years of procurement experience including elements of the supply chain
preferred.
Minimum of five years of negotiation and vendor management experience preferred.
Minimum of five years of customer service experience is required.
Experience in Ivalua, Salesforce, and NetSuite highly desirable.
Relevant industry experience in electrical, security, building automation, or fire is required


Company Benefits And Perks

Convergint fosters a supportive, accessible, and inclusive environment in which individuals of different backgrounds and identities are able to realize their maximum potential within the company. We offer a variety of programs and exceptional benefits to all of our colleagues:


Company Holidays and Paid Time Off
Immediate comprehensive benefit plan (medical, dental, etc.)
Employer contribution to RRSP
Work environment committed to safety.
Colleague Assistance Program
Tuition reimbursement
Competitive salary and compensation plan
Corporate Social Responsibility Day
And much more…


Convergint is committed to a culture of Inclusion and Diversity and is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Visit our Convergint careers site to learn more about the company and the exciting opportunities available.

Title: Data Analysts
Description:
Overview


In this role, you will support the development, management, and analysis of data that drives operational efficiency and informed decision-making. You will work with a variety of data sources and tools to deliver insights, improve processes, and enhance data quality across the organization.


This position offers the opportunity to collaborate with cross-functional teams, contribute to data-driven initiatives, and build expertise in analytics, reporting, and data systems.


Key Responsibilities


Extract, organize, and maintain data from internal systems and external sources

Clean, validate, and format datasets to ensure accuracy and consistency

Assist in analyzing large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights

Support the development of dashboards, reports, and visualizations

Use tools such as Excel, SQL, and Power BI to analyze and present data

Combine datasets and apply formulas or queries to generate meaningful outputs

Assist in automating recurring data processes and improving reporting efficiency

Monitor data for inconsistencies, errors, or missing information

Investigate and resolve data discrepancies or system issues

Ensure data integrity through routine checks and validation processes

Assist with managing user access to data systems and tools

Support data-related workflows, including basic ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) tasks

Participate in improving data processes and documentation

Work with internal teams to understand data needs and support reporting requirements

Assist in responding to data-related inquiries from users and stakeholders

Communicate findings in a clear and user-friendly manner for non-technical audiences

Support small technology or data-related projects and initiatives

Participate in team meetings, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities

Research new tools and methods to improve data analysis and workflows


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Engineering, or a related field (or currently in progress with relevant experience)

3–5 years of experience in a data, IT, or analytical role (co-op or internship experience considered)


Technical Skills


Basic to intermediate proficiency with:

Microsoft Excel (formulas, data organization, pivot tables)

SQL (basic queries and data extraction)

Power BI or similar data visualization tools

Familiarity with databases, data structures, and reporting tools is an asset


Skills & Attributes


Strong attention to detail and data accuracy

Analytical mindset with problem-solving abilities

Ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritize effectively

Strong written and verbal communication skills

Willingness to learn new tools, systems, and technologies

Team-oriented with a proactive attitude








Primus Workforce ltd. provides safety- minded, experienced, professional personnel support throughout the energy, construction and technical trades sectors to our clients' long-term and short-term personnel requirements. We are a growing and dynamic company to work with providing the key to your future success.

Title: Data Analyst, Player & Quality Insights
Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

The Player & Quality Insights team focuses on understanding player behavior and designing player feedback events which help game teams develop high-quality games to meet our player's expectations. We are a data-driven team where every major decision starts with data analysis to find opportunity areas and to measure our efforts. As a Data Analyst, you will oversee and conduct the conversion of data into insights to lead to informed decisions. Reporting to a Manager, Analytics, you will develop new methods to understand our players and directly influence the quality of our games.

Responsibilities


Work with partners to understand project goals and provide clear guidance regarding player behavior and player feedback event design.
Evaluate and interpret data to find risks and opportunities and communicate them with your team and partners.
Build statistical models and validate and improve the performance of these models over time.
Develop scripts and queries to import, clean, transform, and enhance data.
Prepare detailed reports and presentations that help your team identify areas of opportunity and provide insight to guide future playtest events
Promote best practices in tools and visual technologies for building project dashboards that provide important data for project monitoring.


Qualifications


2+ years of work experience in a past analytical role, using data to guide process improvements.
Bachelor's degree in a technical field or completion of post-secondary level Math and Statistics courses.
Excellent foundation of math and statistical knowledge including an understanding of probability, correlation, regression, classification, and the development of statistical models.
Proficient in SQL and ability to extract data from large data sources with differing structures.
Proficient in software tools like Power BI or Tableau to create easy-to-understand data visualizations.
Experience with the Agile/Waterfall processes.
Experience presenting and prioritizing with people at multiple levels of business and technical expertise.


Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$77,700 - $107,900 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

Title: Fleet Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Fleet Analyst to join our team in London. This is a role for a high-agency, analytically strong, and operationally practical analyst/project operator who can turn fleet, maintenance, vendor, and equipment data into clear recommendations, then drive follow-through across mechanics, fleet leadership, vendors, and internal stakeholders.

Pay & Perks

Salary: $65,000 - $85,000 per year base salary.
Annual Performance Bonus: Discretionary performance bonus tied to your results.
Benefits: Group Benefits, including comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage (fully paid by Riverdale after 3 months).
Retirement: RSP/RRSP matching program available.
Additional Perks: 10 days paid vacation, safety boot allowance, holiday lunches, and regular team appreciation events.

Schedule

Full-time: Monday - Friday out of our London yard.
Travel: Some travel as required to inspect equipment and visit other offices and yards across Southwestern Ontario.

What You’ll Do

Asset Planning & Lifecycle Management: Build internal forecasts for asset capacity and retirements, work with operating teams to plan purchases and disposals, perform pre-purchase inspections of prospective trucks with senior mechanics, and manage the onboarding and disposal of equipment.
Analytics & Continuous Improvement: Perform analysis of historical work orders, downtime, and breakdown events to identify root causes and recommend actionable changes to improve future outcomes.
Decision Support: Prepare agendas, conduct analysis, and capture action items for regular steering meetings.
Project Execution: Act as a "safe pair of hands" for ad-hoc fleet tasks. Scope, implement, and monitor continuous improvement projects from start to finish.
System Administration: Build, maintain, and document tools, dashboards, and processes related to fleet operations and equipment.

What You Need

Experience: Background in an operational environment, such as fleet, transportation, logistics, maintenance, or asset management.
Analytical Judgment: Strong problem-solving skills to investigate variances, separate signal from noise, and make practical recommendations.
Tech Proficiency: Strong Excel/spreadsheet modeling skills, and comfort utilizing tools like Google Workspace and Asana. Experience building dashboards is a strong asset.
Resourcefulness & Initiative: You don't sit on your hands waiting for direction; you proactively drive ambiguous work forward and find ways through barriers without constant direction.
Communication: Clear written communication skills to deliver documentation and recommendations, plus the ability to work credibly with mechanics, fleet leadership, and vendors.

Why Riverdale?

We get the job done - the right way (probably the hard way). What you do matters for animal welfare and food security. If you take pride in doing things properly and want to be part of a team that rewards results, we’d love to meet you.

Apply now and we’ll be in touch.

Riverdale Poultry provides accommodation to job applicants with disabilities during the recruitment process. If you require accommodation, please contact Human Resources at hr@riverdalepoultry.ca.

Title: Data Analyst II, Fraud
Description:
Job Description:

At Remitly, we believe everyone deserves the freedom to access, move, and manage their money wherever life takes them. Since 2011, we've tirelessly delivered on our promise to customers sending money globally, providing secure, simple, and reliable ways to manage their money, ensuring true peace of mind.

Whether it's supporting loved ones back home, growing a business across continents, or pursuing new opportunities abroad, we're not just here to move money— we're here to move our global customers forward.

We're looking for builders, reimaginers, and global thinkers who want to work at the intersection of technology, trust, and transformation. If that's you and you're ready to do the most meaningful work of your career—we invite you to join over 2,800 passionate Remitlians worldwide who are united by our vision to transform lives with trusted financial services that transcend borders

About the Role:

As a Data Analyst, Fraud, you will be part of Remitly's Identity & Trust Analytics Team. Your mission is to leverage analytical methods to identify, prevent, and mitigate fraud impacting our customers globally. You will develop a deep understanding of Remitly's fraud detection systems, regional risk profiles, and operational processes to improve fraud defenses while maintaining a seamless experience for legitimate customers. You'll partner with Product, Engineering, Business Management, and Compliance to customize and optimize fraud models and workflows, integrating data from legacy and new platforms. This hybrid role requires 2–3 days per week in the Vancouver office.

You Will:


Become an expert on Remitly's fraud data (e.g. chargebacks, promo abuse, scams, onboarding risk).
Build data-driven models and dashboards to analyze and monitor fraud exposure, balancing customer experience with operational efficiency.
Collect, normalize, and analyze data from legacy prebriefs, transaction logs, and onboarding systems to identify fraud trends and behavior anomalies.
Partner with Business Management and Product to continuously improve customer verification, KYC, KYB and compliance workflows.
Design and evaluate experiments and A/B tests to measure fraud control effectiveness and minimize false positives.
Develop and track risk metrics that quantify fraud exposure, policy compliance, and system performance.
Provide actionable insights and influence the development of fraud detection strategies, tools, and automation.


You Have:


3+ years in an analytics or fraud/risk analysis role within fintech, e-commerce, or digital payments.
Experience with fraud or trust analytics.
Bachelor's degree in mathematics, engineering, economics, or another quantitative discipline (or equivalent experience).
Proficiency with SQL and hands-on experience analyzing large datasets (Python, R, or statistical modeling experience preferred).
Strong understanding of fraud taxonomies (chargebacks, identity theft, scams, onboarding risk, and promo abuse).
Demonstrated ability to distill business problems, develop hypotheses, perform analysis, and communicate clear recommendations.
Excellent data storytelling and communication skills; ability to translate complex findings into business decisions.


Compensation Details. The starting base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $125000. In Canada, Remitly employees are shareholders in our Company and equity is part of our total compensation plan. Your recruiter can share more information about medical benefits offered, as well as other financial benefits and total compensation components offered with this role.

Our Benefits


Four weeks vacation
Health Benefits
Mental Health & Family Forming Benefits
RRSP plan with company match
Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
Life Insurance & Disability
Continuing education and travel benefits


Our Connected Work Culture: Driving Innovation, Together

At Remitly, we believe that true innovation sparks when we come together. Our Connected Work Culture fosters dynamic in-person collaboration, where ideas ignite and challenging problems find solutions faster. For corporate team members, we have an in-office expectation of at least 50% of the time monthly, typically achieved by coming in three days a week. This creates a consistent, meaningful overlap that supports team norms and business needs. Managers also have the flexibility to set higher expectations based on their team's specific needs. These intentional in-office moments are vital for deepening relationships, fueling creativity, and ensuring your impact is felt where it matters most.

Remitly is an E-Verify Employer

At Remitly, we are dedicated to ensuring that our workplace offers equal employment opportunities to all employees and candidates, in full compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

Remitly is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Title: Analytics & Database Manager
Description:
About St. Boniface Hospital Foundation

Founded in 1971, St. Boniface Hospital Foundation raises funds to support excellence in health care and research at St. Boniface Hospital in a manner that is guided by the legacy and values of the Grey Nuns. The Foundation is a family-oriented, caring, and compassionate environment, and we are excited to welcome a new team member!

 

The Opportunity

St. Boniface Hospital Foundation is seeking a data-driven professional who thrives in a collaborative team environment to support our mission in the role of Analytics & Database Manager.

Reporting to the Vice President, Finance and Administration, the Analytics & Database Manager is responsible for overseeing the integrity of the Foundation’s Customer Relationship Management (CRM) database, managing gift processing, including financial reporting and reconciliation, and providing database reporting and analysis. The Analytics & Database Manager is a key role within the organization responsible for generating insights that inform business decisions and leveraging data to drive organizational success.

 

This position requires a strong ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines while ensuring the accurate, timely processing and analysis of donor and fundraising data. If you are naturally curious, detail-oriented, and a results-driven problem-solver who takes pride in data integrity and continuous improvement, this is your opportunity to make a meaningful difference.




SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

 

Minimum of three years of data management and analysis experience using a CRM database, preferably Blackbaud Raisers Edge or NXT.
 Diploma in Data Analysis, Statistics, or a related field and/or equivalent combination of education and experience.
Proven experience in data analysis and ability to track key performance indicators (KPIs) for measuring success.
Accuracy and integrity in managing large data sets.
Commitment to maintain confidentiality and a high degree of accuracy.
Excellent teamwork and interpersonal skills, with the ability to interact effectively with stakeholders within and outside the organization, including donors, volunteers, Board members, and Hospital and Foundation staff.
Demonstrated organization, planning, and problem-solving skills.
Demonstrated success in a multi-tasking, deadline-oriented environment, with the ability to prioritize decisions quickly and accurately.
Bilingualism (French/English) would be an asset.




What We Offer

 

This is a full-time position, with a competitive salary and benefits package, and outstanding work-life balance, including:

A salary range of $75,000 to $90,000, commensurate with experience
Comprehensive group insurance benefits, including pension plan, through Healthcare Employees Benefits (HEB)
Ample access to professional development
The Foundation offers a flexible work schedule and hybrid office environment. All staff are currently expected to work in person at the office a minimum of three days per week
Cell phone provided




If you believe you can make a strong contribution to our team, please send your resume and cover letter to Natalie Domienik, at ndomienik@stbhf.org no later than July 15, 2026.

 

St. Boniface Hospital Foundation is committed to equity and diversity within its community and welcomes applications from racialized persons, persons with disabilities, persons of all sexual and gender identities, and others. If you require accommodations at any point in the hiring process, please let us know and we will be happy to provide them. We appreciate all who apply but only those applicants selected for an interview will be contacted. 




*****************************************************************




À propos de la Fondation de l’Hôpital Saint-Boniface

Depuis sa création en 1971, la Fondation de l’Hôpital Saint-Boniface recueille des fonds pour soutenir l’excellence des soins de santé et de la recherche à l’Hôpital Saint-Boniface en s’inspirant de l’héritage et des valeurs des Sœurs Grises. La Fondation, un milieu de travail où la famille est importante et où règnent la bienveillance et la compassion, se réjouit d’accueillir une nouvelle personne au sein de son équipe!

 

Le poste

La Fondation de l’Hôpital Saint-Boniface est à la recherche d’une personne passionnée par les données et qui s’épanouira dans l’ambiance de collaboration de notre équipe afin de soutenir notre mission en tant que Gestionnaire de la base de données et de l’analytique.




Sous la supervision du vice-président, Finances et administration, le ou la Gestionnaire de la base de données et de l’analytique est responsable de la supervision de l’intégrité de la base de données de gestion des relations avec la clientèle (CRM) de la Fondation, de la gestion du traitement des dons, y compris les rapports financiers et les rapprochements, ainsi que de la production de rapports et d’analyses de données. Cette personne joue un rôle clé au sein de l’organisation en générant des renseignements stratégiques qui éclairent la prise de décision et en tirant parti des données pour contribuer au succès de la Fondation.

 

Ce poste exige une grande capacité à gérer plusieurs priorités et échéances simultanément tout en assurant le traitement et l’analyse exacts et rapides des données liées aux donateurs et aux activités de collecte de fonds. Si vous êtes une personne curieuse de nature, minutieuse, orientée vers les résultats et la résolution de problèmes, et que vous accordez une grande importance à l’intégrité des données et à l’amélioration continue, cette occasion vous permettra d’avoir un réel impact.

 




COMPÉTENCES ET QUALIFICATIONS

 

Minimum de trois années d’expérience en gestion et en analyse de données à l’aide d’une base de données CRM, idéalement Blackbaud Raiser’s Edge ou Raiser’s Edge NXT.
Diplôme en analyse de données, en statistique ou dans un domaine connexe, ou une combinaison équivalente de formation et d’expérience.
Expérience démontrée en analyse de données et capacité à suivre les indicateurs clés de performance (KPI) afin de mesurer le succès des initiatives.
Grande rigueur et souci de l’intégrité dans la gestion de vastes ensembles de données.
Engagement à maintenir la confidentialité et un haut niveau d’exactitude.
Excellentes aptitudes pour le travail d’équipe et les relations interpersonnelles, avec la capacité d’interagir efficacement avec les parties prenantes à l’interne et à l’externe, notamment les donateurs, les bénévoles, les membres du conseil d’administration ainsi que le personnel de l’Hôpital et de la Fondation.
Excellentes compétences en organisation, en planification et en résolution de problèmes.
Capacité démontrée à réussir dans un environnement dynamique comportant de multiples tâches et des échéances serrées, tout en établissant rapidement et efficacement les priorités.
Le bilinguisme (français-anglais) serait un atout.

Ce que nous offrons

Il s’agit d’un poste à temps plein, assorti d’un salaire concurrentiel, d’un ensemble d’avantages sociaux et d’un excellent équilibre travail‑vie personnelle, incluant :

 

Un salaire de 75 000 $ à 90 000 $, selon l’expérience
Des avantages sociaux collectifs complets, y compris un régime de retraite, par l’entremise de Healthcare Employees Benefits (HEB)
De nombreuses possibilités de perfectionnement professionnel
La Fondation offre un horaire de travail flexible et un environnement de travail hybride. Tous les membres du personnel sont actuellement tenus de travailler en présentiel au bureau au moins trois jours par semaine
Téléphone cellulaire fourni

 

La Fondation de l’Hôpital Saint-Boniface offre un salaire et des avantages sociaux concurrentiels, et permet d’atteindre un excellent équilibre entre le travail et la vie personnelle. Le salaire pour ce poste se situe entre 75 000 et 90 000 dollars.

 

Si vous pensez pouvoir apporter une solide contribution à notre équipe, veuillez envoyer votre curriculum vitae et une lettre de motivation à Natalie Domienik, à ndomienik@stbhf.org, au plus tard le 15 juillet 2026.

 

La Fondation de l’Hôpital Saint-Boniface s’engage à respecter les principes d’équité et de diversité au sein de sa communauté et accueillera avec intérêt les candidatures provenant de personnes racisées, de personnes vivant avec un handicap, et de personnes issues de la diversité sexuelle et de genre, entre autres. Si vous avez besoin de mesures d’adaptation à un moment ou à un autre du processus de recrutement, veuillez nous en informer et nous nous ferons un plaisir de vous les fournir. Nous remercions d’avance toutes les personnes qui poseront leur candidature, mais nous ne communiquerons qu’avec les personnes convoquées à une entrevue.

Title: Strategic Finance Analyst (Data & Analytics)
Description:
Closing Date (MM/DD/YYYY)

06/27/2026

Worker Type

Permanent

Language(s) Required

English

Term Duration (in Months)

Salary Range (plus eligible to receive a performance based incentive, applicable to position) :

$94,620 - $128,020

Why FCC?

At FCC, we’re proud to be 100% invested in Canadian agriculture and food. As a federal Crown corporation, we provide financing, knowledge resources and business management software to over 103,000 customers nationwide.

Here’s what you can expect when you join our team:


Competitive total rewards packages: market-aligned and performance-based salary and incentive programs, flexible and comprehensive group benefit and savings plans, and well-being support through benefits and wellness programs
Purpose-driven work: We build strong relationships, share knowledge and support the people who feed the world
Growth: Learning and development opportunities to help you thrive
Hybrid work options



How You’ll Make An Impact

Be part of the team that delivers data-driven insights to support business strategy and works closely with cross-functional stakeholders, including business units, leadership, and data teams.

As a Business Intelligence Analyst, you’ll transform complex data into clear, actionable insights that support business decisions and improve performance across key domains. You’ll play a critical role in helping stakeholders understand trends, identify opportunities, and make informed, data-driven decisions.

We’re looking for someone who thrives in a collaborative environment and builds on others' ideas. An analytical thinker who can use complex concepts and turn them into executive insights and actionable options for better business decision-making. You’re a natural relationship-builder and an active listener, comfortable engaging with diverse stakeholders in any setting—from technical discussions with data teams to strategic conversations with business leaders.

You bring strong problem-solving and communication skills, a passion for data, insights, and continuous improvement, and curiosity, adaptability, and a team-first mindset. Most importantly, you embrace ambiguity, enjoy uncovering patterns in complex data, and have the confidence to create impact from the ground up.

If you thrive in a fast-paced environment, enjoy solving complex problems, and are passionate about turning data into meaningful insights, this could be the role for you.

What You’ll Do


Apply Python in AWS environments to develop dashboards and reporting, bridging business needs with IT-led data solutions
Apply accounting knowledge to interpret results, ensuring insights are grounded in financial statements and meaningful for decision-making
Develop analytics solutions using data from a variety of sources and structures
Analyze data to uncover trends, patterns, and opportunities, and present findings to decision makers
Provide recommendations to improve business processes, data quality, and reporting capabilities
Support continuous improvement by identifying gaps and enhancing data tools and methodologies



Required Qualifications

What you’ll bring to the team


A degree in finance, business, mathematics, statistics, or computer science
Minimum 4 years of experience in data analytics, business analysis, or a related field
Extensive knowledge of data analysis, statistical concepts, and data interpretation
Solid knowledge of business domains combined with data structures and configuration
Proven expertise in translating data into actionable insights and recommendations
Demonstrated proficiency with data manipulation and management tools
Advanced experience with at least one data visualization tool (e.g., Power BI, Tableau)



Preferred Qualifications


Experience working with large and complex datasets from multiple sources
Experience influencing stakeholders and making data-driven recommendations
Knowledge of data governance, data quality, or data process improvement
Experience in a financial institution, working in a finance area or a related industry



You belong here

At FCC, we’re committed to creating an inclusive, equitable and accessible workplace – one that reflects the communities where we live, work and play. Our team is made stronger through diversity, and we’re dedicated to building a workforce that brings together a range of backgrounds, abilities and perspectives.

An HR partner will respond and work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation. Information received in relation to accommodation requests will not impact hiring decisions.

Title: Decision Science Analyst
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. is a certified Great Place to Work® and consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ nearly 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Decision Science Analyst to join our More Rewards team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Customer Data & Analytics, you will be responsible for leveraging all aspects of data and analytics to support large-scale projects, experimentation, and continuous improvement initiatives that guide decision-making and deliver high-quality insights. In this role, you will support the development and management of customer data and insights to support the More Rewards Loyalty Program, Marketing, Retail Operations and Merchandising.

You will be responsible for:


Collaborating with stakeholders to understand business questions, processes and KPIs.
Formulating testable hypotheses aligned with business questions and conducting statistical experiments in a live environment.
Writing SQL scripts to automate data pipelines and workflows.
Using Python scripts in a Jupyter Notebook environment for exploratory data analysis, data wrangling and statistical testing.
Developing and maintaining dashboards and visualizations in Power BI to convey insights, report on KPIs and test results.
Learning cloud-based analytics environments and big data tools to build analytics workflows on large datasets.
Discovering unique and creative insights to identify new opportunities for driving business value.
Becoming an expert in the relevant datasets within your analytics domain.
Supporting the Data & Analytics team with key projects.



You have:


A degree in a STEM discipline with 3+ years of relevant experience in data and analytics.
Advanced Python coding skills and understanding of SQL.
Experience with additional programming languages is an asset.
Experience using experimental design principles such as A/B and multivariate testing and working with business stakeholders to formulate testable hypotheses.
An understanding of advanced analytics and data science methods, such as anomaly detection, forecasting, and supervised & unsupervised learning.
Working knowledge of cloud environments, such as Azure, GCP or AWS. Experience with big data tools such as Spark and Databricks is an asset.



You are:


A confident communicator, both verbally and written, with strong interpersonal skills and the ability to communicate a story with data and insights to non-technical stakeholders.
A problem solver with strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
Effective in task management and delivering high-quality work.
Capable of multitasking and thriving in a fast-paced environment.
Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and collaboratively.



What we offer:

In addition to a highly competitive salary, we offer a great range of benefits, a company pension plan, free parking, exclusive team member offers, and opportunities for development and career progression.

If you’re looking to join a team that puts people first, you’re in the right place. Apply online today!

Compensation Details:

$83,394.00 - $104,243.00

The compensation offered for this position will take into consideration location, education, skills, experience and other factors.

Title: Pricing Analyst
Description:
The Pricing Analyst is instrumental in providing key analytical, maintenance, and costing support to the organization. You will help drive efficiencies within the organization and drive profitable growth into the future.

As the Pricing Analyst you are a real Data Intelligence Analyst. This role is instrumental in providing key analytical, maintenance and costing support to the organization. Specifically, you will become the catalyst which will drive efficiencies within the organization. Your detail-oriented nature will promote and sustain the profitable growth of the company.

Responsibilities


Review market trends for pricing opportunities to optimize revenues
Monitor cost and raw material fluctuations
Interpret data into compelling insights and present to a variety of levels of leadership
Analyze price relationships between products
Manage new product listings in the system with appropriate cost and other key data elements
Assist in development of new key insights by constructing reports which can be utilized throughout the organization
Maintain data integrity within the system by conducting vendor reviews to ensure accurate information is present
Manage vendor price adjustments by uploading data within the system while maintaining accuracy
Maintain accurate vendor contracts and audit monthly denials


Requirements


Bachelor's degree with a specialization in Business or Mathematics
2-3 years previous experience in similar role
Microsoft Office expertise, especially in Excel (v-lookup, index match, Pivot table)
Excellent communicator – speaks and writes clearly and to the point
Analytical and organized with relentless attention to detail
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Team player who displays personal leadership
Basic knowledge of financial statements and trial balance information
Proven track record of process optimization an asset
Ability to work in a fast-paced national distribution environment


Bunzl Canada Inc. (bunzlcanada.ca) provides the food and retail packaging, cleaning and hygiene products and equipment, safety and industrial supplies which keep over 58,000 Canadian businesses running optimally, every day. We bring our customers the advantage of global sourcing, product innovation and national scale combined with responsive local service and deep category expertise. Bunzl Canada is a certified Great Place to Work®. We are committed to creating an inclusive, equitable and positive working environment for all employees so that they experience a strong sense of belonging, while developing individually and professionally. Bunzl Canada is an operating company of Bunzl plc (BNZL.L), a FTSE100 company listed on the London Stock Exchange. With 27,000 employees in over 30 countries, our global presence spans the Americas, Europe, Asia Pacific, and the UK and Ireland. At Bunzl, you’ll find Unlimited Potential… Your Career - - Our Future!

Bunzl Canada has a tradition of commitment to equal employment opportunity. It is our established policy to attract and retain the best qualified people without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex/gender (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, age, disability or veteran status as provided by law.

Title: Master Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a SAP Data Analyst to support a large‑scale SAP implementation, with a primary focus on data migration activities. This role is responsible for analyzing and preparing data for migration, building and maintaining migration objects, executing mock cycles, and supporting data quality, validation, and defect management activities to ensure data readiness for go‑live.




Required Qualifications

3–7 years of experience in data analysis or data migration roles, preferably within SAP or ERP transformation programs.
Experience supporting SAP data migration projects (ECC to S/4HANA preferred)
Hands-on experience with SAP BODS or equivalent ETL tools
Experience building reports / dashboards in MS Power BI
Strong understanding of: Data migration concepts (mapping, cleansing, validation, reconciliation), and Mock cycles and cutover readiness
Advanced Excel skills; experience producing data quality and exception reports
SAP Data Migration – strong understanding of migration concepts (profiling, mapping, cleansing, validation, reconciliation)
Mock Cycle Execution – supporting full mock cycles including re‑runs and stabilization activities
Source‑to‑Target Mapping – maintaining mappings, transformation logic, and validation rules
Data Quality & Reconciliation – producing and analyzing data quality and reconciliation reports
SAP Data Structures – familiarity with SAP ECC and S/4HANA data tables and master data objects

Title: Transportation Data Analyst
Description:
Location: Brampton, Ontario

Job Description

Position: Transportation Data Analyst

Type: Full-Time | Hybrid

Location: Brampton, ON

Reports to: Senior Manager, Inbound Transportation

About Us

Pet Valu is Canada’s leading pet specialty retailer, dedicated to strengthening the bond between dedicated pet lovers and their pets. With 50 years of animal care expertise, we operate over 800 stores across Canada under several banners, including Pet Valu, Bosley’s, Total Pet, Paulmac's Pet, Tisol & Chico. Guided by our mission to deliver products, care, expertise, and memorable moments, Pet Valu is a place where people build meaningful careers while making a positive impact on pets and communities.

What will you do?

Pet Valu’s supply chain has grown significantly as the company has expanded, supported by a $100M investment in supply chain technology and facilities. As part of this investment, Pet Valu launched Manhattan Active Transportation Management to improve network planning, centralize transportation data, and strengthen reporting and operational visibility. Pet Valu’s transportation team is responsible for the movement of all collect inbound freight to the company’s three distribution centres across Canada, and outbound to 800+ stores.

The Transportation Data Analyst is responsible for ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and cost-effectiveness of inbound and outbound transportation data and spend. This role oversees transportation data within the TMS, supports freight audit activities by validating carrier charges and logistics metrics, and develops reporting and KPI insights that drive data-based decision-making. The analyst also helps maintain system configurations, support cost allocation processes, identify opportunities to improve transportation performance and reduce expenses, and partner closely with internal supply chain and IT teams, as well as external freight carriers, to maintain efficient and effective transportation operations across Canada.

Responsibilities

Data Management and Governance


Own the data for the transportation management system by being the subject matter expert for all TMS data, working with IT teams on data flows, and implementing technical business rules, including maintenance carrier, lane, and rate data
Developing and maintain KPI reports for supply chain, and communicate performance results and insights to stakeholders
Manage setup, collection, and analysis of bid data for carrier RFPs


Data Analysis and Reporting


Conduct in-depth data analysis to identify trends, insights, and opportunities for optimization. Develop and maintain comprehensive, automated reports and dashboards using advanced data visualization tools. Present complex data insights to stakeholders in a clear and concise manner
Develop common metrics and data processes that drive business outcomes. Stay abreast of industry trends and best practices in data management and analytics, and recommend innovative solutions to improve data-driven decision-making.


Freight Invoice Audit & Validation


Review and audit outbound freight invoices to ensure accuracy against contracted rates, routing guides, and shipment details
Identify and resolve discrepancies such as incorrect rates, accessorial charges, or duplicate billing
Approve validated invoices for payment processing


Training and Support


Provide training and support to end-users on TMS data tools and best practices. Mentor junior analysts and other team members to enhance their analytical and technical skills.
Vacation / sick time coverage as necessary


Qualifications & Experience


Minimum of 4 years' experience in supply chain / logistics activities
Experience with TMS, WMS, optimization tools, or network modeling preferred
Experience with Excel, PowerBI, Access, Cognos, Snowflake, and other database and analytics tools
Experience with data analysis and building KPI tools


Compensation

The targeted salary range for this position is $65,000-$75,000 annually. The final offer will be based on factors such as market location, relevant skills, experience and internal equity.

Ready to join the team?

We’re looking for passionate people who bring strong skills and live our core values of safety, compassion, expertise, and efficiency every day. If you’re energized by doing meaningful work, taking pride in what you deliver, and making a real impact for pets, people, and communities, you’ll feel right at home here. Join us and build a career where purpose, care, and excellence come together - because love lives here™.

Why work for us?

About

At Pet Valu, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We’re passionate about creating an environment where you feel supported, included, and empowered to grow - both personally and professionally. Some of the perks of working with us include:


Hybrid, Flexible Work Options
Comprehensive health and dental benefits
Employee Share Purchase Plan with company matching
Learning and Development support tailored to you
An inclusive, collaborative culture where your voice matters
Fun company events that keep us connected
Preferred rates for car rentals, hotels, phone plans and gym discounts
Generous employee discounts on our products


Pet Valu is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and accessibility. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the recruitment and selection process, in accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).

At Pet Valu, we are committed to transparency and fairness in our hiring practices. This posting is for an existing vacancy. Pet Valu may use AI enabled technology to assist in the recruitment process for positions. The use of AI helps us ensure we are providing an equitable and streamlined recruitment experience for all applicants. While AI tools support our recruitment process, all decisions throughout the process are made exclusively by Pet Valu’s hiring team. At no stage does AI determine recruitment outcomes.

Title: Data Management
Description:
Data Management


Overview


In this role, you will be responsible for managing and analyzing capital project data to support budgeting, forecasting, and long-term planning. You will develop and maintain tools, models, and reporting systems that provide visibility into capital performance and ensure alignment with regulatory, financial, and operational requirements.


Working closely with Finance, Project Management, and Planning teams, you will play a key role in enabling data-driven decisions, strengthening governance practices, and improving the accuracy and efficiency of capital reporting processes.


Key Responsibilities


Support capital planning, budget development, and forecasting activities

Continuously enhance reporting capabilities through automation and process improvements

Strong knowledge of:

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and research capabilities

Title: Content Analyst
Description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

The team:

We are a unified, collaborative marketing team focused on creating meaningful client connections that drive business growth. We combine deep insights with strong partnerships to deliver timely, relevant, and impactful experiences for our clients. Our team is committed to continuous learning, innovation, and excellence in everything we do. We align closely with distribution partners to generate demand and unlock new opportunities across the business. Above all, we measure and optimize our efforts to ensure we consistently deliver value, drive engagement, and strengthen loyalty to Invesco.

The role:

The Content Enablement Content Analyst is a key role within Invesco’s Global Content Enablement team, supporting the Americas & EMEA Marketing organization. This position executes the content strategy and roadmap to enhance the quality, accessibility, and usability of content across the EMEA region. The role focuses on building and managing content infrastructure, ensuring accurate categorization, distribution, and access across platforms and audiences. It partners closely with business and technology stakeholders to deliver consistent, well-governed content solutions and ensure requirements are clearly defined and tested. This hands-on role is critical to improving content quality and driving engagement, ultimately enabling a stronger client and investment experience.

Responsibilities of the role:

Provides analytics support primarily for EMEA content domains, with additional support for North America.
Partners closely with EMEA Content Product Owners to deliver analysis across development, deployment, and research initiatives.
Defines and standardizes content quality metrics, building reporting and dashboards to provide visibility into content performance for senior leadership.
Collaborates with EMEA and NA domain experts to develop and maintain global taxonomy, aligning stakeholders to standardized definitions and fields.
Bridges business and technology teams to ensure solutions meet requirements and align with strategic priorities.
Maps business processes, develops expertise in content sources, and provides recommendations to improve content effectiveness.
Translates stakeholder needs into user stories and supports execution of content initiatives, including testing, impact analysis, and cross-team coordination.
Leads governance and documentation efforts, including content quality monitoring, process improvement, issue escalation, and maintaining records in tools such as Jira and Confluence.


Requirements of the role:

Bachelor’s degree, 5+ years in technology platforms, project management, or content development and distribution.
Experience with CMS and/or DAM platforms, content lifecycle management, and content migrations, including API-based integrations.
Familiarity with AI-assisted content analysis and UX or digital marketing concepts.
Strong skills in workflow/process mapping, with the ability to manage multiple priorities, meet deadlines, and operate effectively in ambiguous environments.
Advanced technical proficiency, including SQL and Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables; VBA a plus), along with strong desktop tool capabilities.
Strong analytical and statistical reasoning skills, with ability to organize, analyze, and communicate large data sets with accuracy and attention to detail.
Knowledge of content categorization (campaign, program, asset class, product), with preferred experience in investment or asset management content.
Client-focused, execution-oriented professional with strong communication, organizational, relationship-building, and influencing skills, plus a data-driven approach to measuring content performance and impact.


The salary range for this position is $55,000 to $61,000 per year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success and we strive to provide employees with a competitive total rewards package which includes:

Hybrid working environment
RRSP Contributions
Health & Wellness Benefits
Work flexibility Programs
Parental Leave Benefits
Study Support


The above information on this description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this role. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The job holder may be required to perform other duties as deemed appropriate by their manager from time to time.

Invesco is committed to fair and accessible employment practices. If selected for an interview, we will work with you to ensure that your interview is accessible and accommodation is provided. Please contact us at accessibility@invesco.ca or 1.800.874.6275 to let us know if you require accommodation for an interview due to a disability.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
SaskEnergy is seeking a qualified and motivated individual to join our team. The  Senior Data Analyst utilizes quantitative and qualitative research and analysis to develop and monitor operational benchmarks and recommend strategies to improve operational effectiveness. The Senior Data Analyst will build, maintain and support the enterprise reporting layer to provide users with efficient and functional tools and data that can provide business insights and optimize data driven decision making.  

  

Position Details


Non-union. This position is Temporary full time for up to 12 months.


Key Accountabilities


Reporting, analysis and interpretation of performance indicators, internal benchmarks and opportunities for stakeholders, including accessing and analyzing confidential or sensitive information. 
Provide analytical insight to the dependencies and interrelationships between data and benchmarks to recommend business efficiencies and inform business strategy.  
Provide strategic operating intelligence and business metrics for stakeholders to enable data driven decision making and reporting at the department and corporate levels.  
Lead the identification and implementation of data-based solutions for complex business and technical challenges. 
Liaise with a wide range of stakeholders such as subject matter experts, management, and executives to determine data needs, provide data analysis and present business insights. 
Collaborate with business units to develop robust research questions and contribute to all steps of the data analysis process, including hypothesis generation, exploring data, modelling/interpreting/communicating results, and measuring their implementation. 
Ensure knowledge sharing and management of current data analytics artifacts. 
Research and design forecasting and modeling tools to identify operating trends, identifies anomalies, probable impacts, and recommends corrective actions.  
Research, develop, design, implement and maintain internal reporting datasets (semantic layer) as well as other data driven applications.  
Lead the implementation, development and designs in corporate Business Intelligence Tools. 
Other duties as assigned.  


Knowledge/Skills/Abilities


A recognized bachelor’s degree in computer science, mathematics, statistics, engineering, or a related quantitative discipline. 
Five (5) years in reporting or analytics role. 
Advanced knowledge of SQL and experience working with relational databases, query authoring (SQL) as well as working familiarity with a variety of databases. 
Significant experience with Power BI, SQL Server and SSIS. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills for coordinating across teams and presenting ideas to stakeholders. 
Strong facilitation, analytical and problem-solving skills. 
Experience in Python, big data tools, and cloud services considered an asset. 
Ability to work on a team while building and maintaining effective relationships. 


A suitable combination of relevant education and experience may also be considered. 

Application Instructions


Please apply online with your cover letter and resume outlining your qualifications.
For more information on this position, please contact Human Resources at 306-777-9091.


Closing Date

June 26, 2026

Salary Range

$81,613.00 - $117,050.00
